   558→            }
   559→
   560→            if (mappingsData.size > MAX_IMPORT_ROWS) {
   561→                throw IllegalArgumentException(
   562→                    "File contains ${mappingsData.size} records, exceeding maximum of $MAX_IMPORT_ROWS. " +
   563→                    "Split the file into smaller batches."
   564→                )
   565→            }
   566→
   567→            mappingsData.forEach { mapping ->
   568→                val email = (mapping["email"] ?: mapping["Email"]) as? String
   569→                val domains = (mapping["domains"] ?: mapping["Domains"]) as? List<*>
   570→                // Support both standard format ("awsAccounts": ["123..."]) and
   571→                // Cloud Custodian format ("account_id": "123...")
   572→                val awsAccounts = (mapping["awsAccounts"] ?: mapping["AwsAccounts"]) as? List<*>
   573→                    ?: (mapping["account_id"] as? String)?.let { listOf(it) }
   574→
   575→                if (email.isNullOrBlank()) {
   576→                    results.add(
   577→                        MappingOperationResult(
   578→                            success = false,
   579→                            operation = "ERROR",
   580→                            message = "Missing or invalid email field",
   581→                            email = "",
   582→                            domain = null,
   583→                            awsAccountId = null
   584→                        )
   585→                    )
   586→                    return@forEach
   587→                }
   588→
   589→                // Process domains
   590→                domains?.forEach { domain ->
   591→                    val domainStr = domain.toString()
   592→                    val result = if (!dryRun) {
   593→                        createMapping(
   594→                            email = email.trim(),
   595→                            domain = domainStr.trim(),
   596→                            awsAccountId = null,
   597→                            adminEmail = adminEmail,
   598→                            operationType = "IMPORT_JSON_DOMAIN"
   599→                        )
   600→                    } else {
   601→                        if (!emailRegex.matches(email.trim())) {
   602→                            MappingOperationResult(
   603→                                success = false,
   604→                                operation = "ERROR",
   605→                                message = "Invalid email format",
   606→                                email = email.trim(),
   607→                                domain = domainStr.trim()
   608→                            )
   609→                        } else if (!domainRegex.matches(domainStr.trim())) {
   610→                            MappingOperationResult(
   611→                                success = false,
   612→                                operation = "ERROR",
   613→                                message = "Invalid domain format",
   614→                                email = email.trim(),
   615→                                domain = domainStr.trim()
   616→                            )
   617→                        } else {
   618→                            MappingOperationResult(
   619→                                success = true,
   620→                                operation = "WOULD_CREATE",
   621→                                message = "Would create mapping (dry-run)",
   622→                                email = email.trim(),
   623→                                domain = domainStr.trim()
   624→                            )
   625→                        }
   626→                    }
   627→                    results.add(result)
   628→                }
   629→
   630→                // Process AWS accounts
   631→                awsAccounts?.forEach { account ->
   632→                    val accountStr = account.toString()
   633→                    val result = if (!dryRun) {
   634→                        createMapping(
   635→                            email = email.trim(),
   636→                            domain = null,
   637→                            awsAccountId = accountStr.trim(),
   638→                            adminEmail = adminEmail,
   639→                            operationType = "IMPORT_JSON_AWS"
   640→                        )
   641→                    } else {
   642→                        if (!emailRegex.matches(email.trim())) {
   643→                            MappingOperationResult(
   644→                                success = false,
   645→                                operation = "ERROR",
   646→                                message = "Invalid email format",
   647→                                email = email.trim(),
   648→                                awsAccountId = accountStr.trim()
   649→                            )
   650→                        } else if (!awsAccountIdRegex.matches(accountStr.trim())) {
   651→                            MappingOperationResult(
   652→                                success = false,
   653→                                operation = "ERROR",
   654→                                message = "Invalid AWS account ID (must be 12 digits)",
   655→                                email = email.trim(),
   656→                                awsAccountId = accountStr.trim()
   657→                            )
   658→                        } else {
   659→                            MappingOperationResult(
   660→                                success = true,
   661→                                operation = "WOULD_CREATE",
   662→                                message = "Would create mapping (dry-run)",
   663→                                email = email.trim(),
   664→                                awsAccountId = accountStr.trim()
   665→                            )
   666→                        }
   667→                    }
   668→                    results.add(result)
   669→                }
   670→            }
   671→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   672→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Failed to parse JSON file: ${e.message}", e)
   673→        }
   674→
   675→        return summarizeResults(results)
   676→    }
   677→
   678→    /**
   679→     * Compare parsed file mappings against existing DB mappings (dry-run enhancement)
   680→     *
   681→     * Uses normalized key tuple (email, awsAccountId, domain) matching the entity's
   682→     * @PrePersist normalization and unique constraint. Falls back gracefully if DB
   683→     * is unreachable (initialization-fail-timeout: -1 allows startup without DB).
   684→     */
   685→    private fun compareMappingsWithDb(operations: List<MappingOperationResult>): MappingComparisonResult {
   686→        try {
   687→            // Build file keys from successfully parsed operations only
   688→            val fileKeys = operations
   689→                .filter { it.success }
   690→                .map { Triple(it.email.lowercase().trim(), it.awsAccountId?.trim(), it.domain?.lowercase()?.trim()) }
   691→                .toSet()
   692→
   693→            // Load all DB mappings and filter to AWS/domain only (exclude IP-only mappings)
   694→            val dbMappings = userMappingRepository.findAll()
   695→                .filter { it.awsAccountId != null || it.domain != null }
   696→
   697→            val dbKeys = dbMappings
   698→                .map { Triple(it.email.lowercase().trim(), it.awsAccountId?.trim(), it.domain?.lowercase()?.trim()) }
   699→                .toSet()
   700→
   701→            val newKeys = fileKeys - dbKeys
   702→            val unchangedKeys = fileKeys.intersect(dbKeys)
   703→            val removedKeys = dbKeys - fileKeys
   704→
   705→            return MappingComparisonResult(
   706→                dbMappingCount = dbKeys.size,
   707→                fileMappingCount = fileKeys.size,
   708→                newCount = newKeys.size,
   709→                unchangedCount = unchangedKeys.size,
   710→                removedCount = removedKeys.size,
   711→                dbAvailable = true
   712→            )
   713→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   714→            log.warn("Could not query database for mapping comparison: ${e.message}")
   715→            return MappingComparisonResult(
   716→                dbMappingCount = 0,
   717→                fileMappingCount = operations.count { it.success },
   718→                newCount = 0,
   719→                unchangedCount = 0,
   720→                removedCount = 0,
   721→                dbAvailable = false
   722→            )
   723→        }
   724→    }
   725→
   726→    /**
   727→     * Summarize operation results into counts
   728→     */
   729→    private fun summarizeResults(results: List<MappingOperationResult>): MappingResult {
   730→        val created = results.count { it.operation == "CREATED" && !it.isPending }
   731→        val createdPending = results.count { it.operation == "CREATED" && it.isPending }
   732→        val skipped = results.count { it.operation == "SKIPPED_DUPLICATE" }
   733→        val errors = results.filter { it.operation == "ERROR" }
   734→
   735→        return MappingResult(
   736→            totalProcessed = results.size,
   737→            created = created,
   738→            createdPending = createdPending,
   739→            skipped = skipped,
   740→            errors = errors.map { "${it.email} → ${it.domain ?: it.awsAccountId}: ${it.message}" },
   741→            operations = results
   742→        )
   743→    }
   744→}
   745→
   746→/**
   747→ * Result of a single mapping operation
   748→ */
   749→data class MappingOperationResult(
   750→    val success: Boolean,
   751→    val operation: String, // CREATED, SKIPPED_DUPLICATE, ERROR
   752→    val message: String,
   753→    val email: String,
   754→    val domain: String? = null,
   755→    val awsAccountId: String? = null,
   756→    val isPending: Boolean = false
   757→)
   758→
   759→/**
   760→ * Summary of multiple mapping operations
   761→ */
   762→data class MappingResult(
   763→    val totalProcessed: Int,
   764→    val created: Int,
   765→    val createdPending: Int = 0,
   766→    val skipped: Int,
   767→    val errors: List<String>,
   768→    val operations: List<MappingOperationResult>,
   769→    val comparison: MappingComparisonResult? = null
   770→)
   771→
   772→/**
   773→ * Result of comparing file mappings against the database (dry-run only)
   774→ */
   775→data class MappingComparisonResult(
   776→    val dbMappingCount: Int,
   777→    val fileMappingCount: Int,
   778→    val newCount: Int,
   779→    val unchangedCount: Int,
   780→    val removedCount: Int,
   781→    val dbAvailable: Boolean
   782→)
   783→

1→package com.secman.cli.service
     2→
     3→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     4→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
     5→import software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.AwsBasicCredentials
     6→import software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.AwsSessionCredentials
     7→import software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.DefaultCredentialsProvider
     8→import software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.ProfileCredentialsProvider
     9→import software.amazon.awssdk.auth.credentials.StaticCredentialsProvider
    10→import software.amazon.awssdk.core.exception.SdkClientException
    11→import software.amazon.awssdk.regions.Region
    12→import software.amazon.awssdk.services.s3.S3Client
    13→import software.amazon.awssdk.services.s3.model.*
    14→import software.amazon.awssdk.services.s3.model.ListObjectsV2Request
    15→import java.nio.file.Files
    16→import java.nio.file.Path
    17→import java.nio.file.attribute.PosixFilePermission
    18→import java.time.Instant
    19→
    20→/**
    21→ * Service for downloading files from AWS S3 (Feature 065)
    22→ *
    23→ * Provides S3 download functionality with:
    24→ * - Standard AWS credential chain support
    25→ * - Named profile support
    26→ * - Region configuration
    27→ * - File size validation (10MB limit)
    28→ * - User-friendly error messages
    29→ * - Automatic temp file cleanup
    30→ */
    31→@Singleton
    32→class S3DownloadService {
    33→
    34→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(S3DownloadService::class.java)
    35→
    36→    companion object {
    37→        const val MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES: Long = 10 * 1024 * 1024 // 10MB
    38→        const val MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB = 10
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    /**
    42→     * Download an S3 object to a temporary file
    43→     *
    44→     * @param bucket S3 bucket name
    45→     * @param key S3 object key (path)
    46→     * @param region AWS region (null = use SDK default resolution)
    47→     * @param profile AWS credential profile name (null = use default credential chain)
    48→     * @param accessKeyId AWS access key ID (null = use profile or default credential chain)
    49→     * @param secretAccessKey AWS secret access key (required when accessKeyId is provided)
    50→     * @param sessionToken AWS session token for temporary credentials (optional, used with accessKeyId)
    51→     * @return Path to downloaded temporary file (caller must delete when done)
    52→     * @throws S3DownloadException on S3 or authentication errors
    53→     */
    54→    fun downloadToTempFile(
    55→        bucket: String,
    56→        key: String,
    57→        region: String? = null,
    58→        profile: String? = null,
    59→        accessKeyId: String? = null,
    60→        secretAccessKey: String? = null,
    61→        sessionToken: String? = null
    62→    ): Path {
    63→        validateBucketName(bucket)
    64→        validateObjectKey(key)
    65→
    66→        val s3Client = buildS3Client(region, profile, accessKeyId, secretAccessKey, sessionToken)
    67→
    68→        try {
    69→            // Check file size before downloading (best-effort — requires s3:HeadObject)
    70→            val objectSize = try {
    71→                getObjectSize(s3Client, bucket, key)
    72→            } catch (e: S3Exception) {
    73→                if (e.statusCode() == 403) {
    74→                    log.warn("HeadObject permission denied — skipping pre-download size check. " +
    75→                        "Grant s3:HeadObject for file size validation before download.")
    76→                    null
    77→                } else {
    78→                    throw e  // re-throw non-permission errors (e.g. 404 will be caught by outer handler)
    79→                }
    80→            }
    81→
    82→            if (objectSize != null && objectSize > MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES) {
    83→                throw S3DownloadException(
    84→                    "File 's3://$bucket/$key' exceeds ${MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB}MB limit (actual: ${objectSize / 1024 / 1024}MB)"
    85→                )
    86→            }
    87→
    88→            // Create temp file with appropriate extension
    89→            val extension = getFileExtension(key)
    90→            val tempFile = Files.createTempFile("s3-import-", extension)
    91→
    92→            // Restrict temp file to owner-only access (rw-------)
    93→            try {
    94→                Files.setPosixFilePermissions(tempFile, setOf(
    95→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ,
    96→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE
    97→                ))
    98→            } catch (e: UnsupportedOperationException) {
    99→                log.debug("POSIX file permissions not supported on this platform")
   100→            }
   101→
   102→            log.info("Downloading s3://$bucket/$key to temp file: $tempFile")
   103→
   104→            try {
   105→                // Use streaming getObject instead of path-based overload to avoid
   106→                // SDK FileResponseTransformer issues with pre-existing temp files
   107→                s3Client.getObject(
   108→                    GetObjectRequest.builder()
   109→                        .bucket(bucket)
   110→                        .key(key)
   111→                        .build()
   112→                ).use { responseStream ->
   113→                    Files.copy(responseStream, tempFile, java.nio.file.StandardCopyOption.REPLACE_EXISTING)
   114→                }
   115→
   116→                // Post-download size check (catches oversized files when HeadObject was unavailable)
   117→                val downloadedSize = Files.size(tempFile)
   118→                if (downloadedSize > MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES) {
   119→                    Files.deleteIfExists(tempFile)
   120→                    throw S3DownloadException(
   121→                        "Downloaded file exceeds ${MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB}MB limit (actual: ${downloadedSize / 1024 / 1024}MB). File deleted."
   122→                    )
   123→                }
   124→
   125→                log.info("Downloaded ${downloadedSize / 1024}KB from S3")
   126→                return tempFile
   127→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   128→                // Clean up temp file on download failure
   129→                Files.deleteIfExists(tempFile)
   130→                throw e
   131→            }
   132→
   133→        } catch (e: NoSuchBucketException) {
   134→            throw S3DownloadException("Bucket '$bucket' does not exist or is not accessible")
   135→        } catch (e: NoSuchKeyException) {
   136→            throw S3DownloadException("File 's3://$bucket/$key' not found")
   137→        } catch (e: S3Exception) {
   138→            if (e.statusCode() == 403) {
   139→                throw S3DownloadException(
   140→                    "Access denied. Check IAM permissions for bucket '$bucket'. " +
   141→                    "Required permission: s3:GetObject"
   142→                )
   143→            }
   144→            throw S3DownloadException("S3 error: ${e.awsErrorDetails()?.errorMessage() ?: e.message}")
   145→        } catch (e: SdkClientException) {
   146→            val message = e.message ?: "Unknown error"
   147→            when {
   148→                message.contains("credentials", ignoreCase = true) ||
   149→                message.contains("Unable to load", ignoreCase = true) -> {
   150→                    throw S3DownloadException(
   151→                        "AWS credentials not found. Configure via:\n" +
   152→                        "  - Environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)\n" +
   153→                        "  - AWS credentials file (~/.aws/credentials)\n" +
   154→                        "  - IAM role (for EC2/ECS deployments)\n" +
   155→                        "  - Use --aws-profile to specify a named profile"
   156→                    )
   157→                }
   158→                message.contains("network", ignoreCase = true) ||
   159→                message.contains("connect", ignoreCase = true) ||
   160→                message.contains("timeout", ignoreCase = true) -> {
   161→                    throw S3DownloadException(
   162→                        "Network error connecting to S3. Check:\n" +
   163→                        "  - Internet connectivity\n" +
   164→                        "  - AWS region (use --aws-region if bucket is in a different region)\n" +
   165→                        "  - Firewall/proxy settings"
   166→                    )
   167→                }
   168→                else -> throw S3DownloadException("S3 client error: $message")
   169→            }
   170→        } catch (e: S3DownloadException) {
   171→            throw e
   172→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   173→            log.error("Unexpected error downloading from S3", e)
   174→            throw S3DownloadException("Unexpected error: ${e.message}")
   175→        } finally {
   176→            s3Client.close()
   177→        }
   178→    }
   179→
   180→    /**
   181→     * Build S3Client with appropriate credentials and region
   182→     *
   183→     * Credential resolution priority:
   184→     * 1. Explicit credentials (accessKeyId + secretAccessKey + optional sessionToken) - highest priority
   185→     * 2. Named profile (--aws-profile)
   186→     * 3. Default credential chain (env vars, ~/.aws/credentials, IAM role) - lowest priority
   187→     */
   188→    private fun buildS3Client(
   189→        region: String?,
   190→        profile: String?,
   191→        accessKeyId: String? = null,
   192→        secretAccessKey: String? = null,
   193→        sessionToken: String? = null
   194→    ): S3Client {
   195→        val builder = S3Client.builder()
   196→
   197→        // Configure credentials provider (explicit > profile > default chain)
   198→        when {
   199→            accessKeyId != null && secretAccessKey != null -> {
   200→                log.debug("Using explicit AWS credentials (access key ID: ${accessKeyId.take(4)}...)")
   201→                // ASIA prefix = temporary STS credentials; session token is mandatory
   202→                if (accessKeyId.startsWith("ASIA") && sessionToken == null) {
   203→                    throw S3DownloadException(
   204→                        "Temporary AWS credentials detected (ASIA-prefix key) but no session token provided.\n" +
   205→                        "  Temporary/STS credentials require all three parts:\n" +
   206→                        "  - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID (or --aws-access-key-id)\n" +
   207→                        "  - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (or --aws-secret-access-key)\n" +
   208→                        "  - AWS_SESSION_TOKEN (or --aws-session-token)"
   209→                    )
   210→                }
   211→                val credentials = if (sessionToken != null) {
   212→                    log.debug("Using session token for temporary credentials")
   213→                    AwsSessionCredentials.create(accessKeyId, secretAccessKey, sessionToken)
   214→                } else {
   215→                    AwsBasicCredentials.create(accessKeyId, secretAccessKey)
   216→                }
   217→                builder.credentialsProvider(StaticCredentialsProvider.create(credentials))
   218→            }
   219→            accessKeyId != null && secretAccessKey == null -> {
   220→                throw S3DownloadException(
   221→                    "AWS access key ID provided but secret access key is missing.\n" +
   222→                    "  Both --aws-access-key-id and --aws-secret-access-key are required together.\n" +
   223→                    "  Alternatively, set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY environment variables."
   224→                )
   225→            }
   226→            profile != null -> {
   227→                log.debug("Using AWS profile: $profile")
   228→                builder.credentialsProvider(ProfileCredentialsProvider.create(profile))
   229→            }
   230→            else -> {
   231→                log.debug("Using default AWS credential chain")
   232→                builder.credentialsProvider(DefaultCredentialsProvider.create())
   233→            }
   234→        }
   235→
   236→        // Configure region
   237→        if (region != null) {
   238→            log.debug("Using explicit AWS region: $region")
   239→            builder.region(Region.of(region))
   240→        }
   241→        // If region is null, SDK will resolve from:
   242→        // 1. AWS_REGION environment variable
   243→        // 2. AWS config file (~/.aws/config)
   244→        // 3. EC2 instance metadata
   245→
   246→        return builder.build()
   247→    }
   248→
   249→    /**
   250→     * Get object size via HeadObject request
   251→     */
   252→    private fun getObjectSize(s3Client: S3Client, bucket: String, key: String): Long {
   253→        val headResponse = s3Client.headObject(
   254→            HeadObjectRequest.builder()
   255→                .bucket(bucket)
   256→                .key(key)
   257→                .build()
   258→        )
   259→        return headResponse.contentLength()
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    /**
   263→     * Validate bucket name format
   264→     * AWS bucket names: 3-63 chars, lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, periods
   265→     */
   266→    private fun validateBucketName(bucket: String) {
   267→        // Detect common mistake: passing an AWS Console URL instead of a bucket name
   268→        if (bucket.startsWith("http://") || bucket.startsWith("https://")) {
   269→            val extractedName = extractBucketNameFromUrl(bucket)
   270→            if (extractedName != null) {
   271→                throw S3DownloadException(
   272→                    "Invalid bucket name: you passed an AWS Console URL.\n" +
   273→                    "  The bucket name appears to be: $extractedName\n" +
   274→                    "  Try: --bucket $extractedName"
   275→                )
   276→            } else {
   277→                throw S3DownloadException(
   278→                    "Invalid bucket name: you passed a URL instead of a bucket name.\n" +
   279→                    "  Use just the bucket name, e.g.: --bucket my-bucket-name\n" +
   280→                    "  You can find the bucket name in the S3 console URL path after /buckets/"
   281→                )
   282→            }
   283→        }
   284→
   285→        // Detect ARN format
   286→        if (bucket.startsWith("arn:")) {
   287→            throw S3DownloadException(
   288→                "Invalid bucket name: you passed an S3 ARN.\n" +
   289→                "  Use just the bucket name, e.g.: --bucket my-bucket-name\n" +
   290→                "  The bucket name is the part after the last ':' or '/' in the ARN."
   291→            )
   292→        }
   293→
   294→        // Detect s3:// URI format
   295→        if (bucket.startsWith("s3://")) {
   296→            val name = bucket.removePrefix("s3://").trimEnd('/')
   297→            throw S3DownloadException(
   298→                "Invalid bucket name: you passed an S3 URI.\n" +
   299→                "  Use just the bucket name: --bucket $name"
   300→            )
   301→        }
   302→
   303→        if (bucket.isBlank()) {
   304→            throw S3DownloadException("Invalid bucket name: cannot be empty")
   305→        }
   306→        if (bucket.length < 3 || bucket.length > 63) {
   307→            throw S3DownloadException(
   308→                "Invalid bucket name '$bucket': must be 3-63 characters (got ${bucket.length})"
   309→            )
   310→        }
   311→        if (!bucket.matches(Regex("^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.-]*[a-z0-9]$"))) {
   312→            throw S3DownloadException(
   313→                "Invalid bucket name '$bucket': must start and end with lowercase letter or number, " +
   314→                "contain only lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and periods"
   315→            )
   316→        }
   317→    }
   318→
   319→    /**
   320→     * Try to extract a bucket name from an AWS Console URL.
   321→     * Handles URLs like:
   322→     *   https://s3.console.aws.amazon.com/s3/buckets/my-bucket?region=us-east-1
   323→     *   https://123456789012.eu-central-1.console.aws.amazon.com/s3/buckets/my-bucket?region=eu-central-1
   324→     */
   325→    private fun extractBucketNameFromUrl(url: String): String? {
   326→        val regex = Regex("/s3/buckets/([^?/]+)")
   327→        return regex.find(url)?.groupValues?.get(1)
   328→    }
   329→
   330→    /**
   331→     * Validate prefix for listing operations
   332→     * Same constraints as object keys: max 1024 chars, no null bytes
   333→     */
   334→    private fun validatePrefix(prefix: String) {
   335→        if (prefix.length > 1024) {
   336→            throw S3DownloadException("Invalid prefix: exceeds 1024 character limit")
   337→        }
   338→        if (prefix.contains('\u0000')) {
   339→            throw S3DownloadException("Invalid prefix: cannot contain null characters")
   340→        }
   341→    }
   342→
   343→    /**
   344→     * Validate object key
   345→     * AWS keys can be up to 1024 bytes, should not contain null bytes
   346→     */
   347→    private fun validateObjectKey(key: String) {
   348→        if (key.isEmpty()) {
   349→            throw S3DownloadException("Invalid object key: cannot be empty")
   350→        }
   351→        if (key.length > 1024) {
   352→            throw S3DownloadException("Invalid object key: exceeds 1024 character limit")
   353→        }
   354→        if (key.contains('\u0000')) {
   355→            throw S3DownloadException("Invalid object key: cannot contain null characters")
   356→        }
   357→    }
   358→
   359→    /**
   360→     * Extract file extension from S3 key
   361→     */
   362→    private fun getFileExtension(key: String): String {
   363→        val lastDot = key.lastIndexOf('.')
   364→        return if (lastDot > 0 && lastDot < key.length - 1) {
   365→            ".${key.substring(lastDot + 1)}"
   366→        } else {
   367→            ".tmp"
   368→        }
   369→    }
   370→
   371→    /**
   372→     * List objects in an S3 bucket with optional prefix filter
   373→     *
   374→     * @param bucket S3 bucket name
   375→     * @param prefix Optional key prefix to filter results
   376→     * @param region AWS region (null = use SDK default resolution)
   377→     * @param profile AWS credential profile name (null = use default credential chain)
   378→     * @param accessKeyId AWS access key ID (null = use profile or default credential chain)
   379→     * @param secretAccessKey AWS secret access key (required when accessKeyId is provided)
   380→     * @param sessionToken AWS session token for temporary credentials (optional, used with accessKeyId)
   381→     * @return List of S3ObjectInfo for matching objects
   382→     * @throws S3DownloadException on S3 or authentication errors
   383→     */
   384→    fun listObjects(
   385→        bucket: String,
   386→        prefix: String? = null,
   387→        region: String? = null,
   388→        profile: String? = null,
   389→        accessKeyId: String? = null,
   390→        secretAccessKey: String? = null,
   391→        sessionToken: String? = null
   392→    ): List<S3ObjectInfo> {
   393→        validateBucketName(bucket)
   394→        if (prefix != null) {
   395→            validatePrefix(prefix)
   396→        }
   397→
   398→        val s3Client = buildS3Client(region, profile, accessKeyId, secretAccessKey, sessionToken)
   399→
   400→        try {
   401→            val requestBuilder = ListObjectsV2Request.builder()
   402→                .bucket(bucket)
   403→                .maxKeys(1000)
   404→            if (prefix != null) {
   405→                requestBuilder.prefix(prefix)
   406→            }
   407→
   408→            val response = s3Client.listObjectsV2(requestBuilder.build())
   409→
   410→            if (response.isTruncated) {
   411→                log.warn("Results truncated: bucket contains more than 1,000 objects matching the prefix. Use a more specific --prefix to narrow results.")
   412→            }
   413→
   414→            return response.contents().map { obj ->
   415→                S3ObjectInfo(
   416→                    key = obj.key(),
   417→                    size = obj.size(),
   418→                    lastModified = obj.lastModified()
   419→                )
   420→            }
   421→        } catch (e: NoSuchBucketException) {
   422→            throw S3DownloadException("Bucket '$bucket' does not exist or is not accessible")
   423→        } catch (e: S3Exception) {
   424→            if (e.statusCode() == 403) {
   425→                throw S3DownloadException(
   426→                    "Access denied. Check IAM permissions for bucket '$bucket'. " +
   427→                    "Required permissions: s3:ListBucket"
   428→                )
   429→            }
   430→            throw S3DownloadException("S3 error: ${e.awsErrorDetails()?.errorMessage() ?: e.message}")
   431→        } catch (e: SdkClientException) {
   432→            val message = e.message ?: "Unknown error"
   433→            when {
   434→                message.contains("credentials", ignoreCase = true) ||
   435→                message.contains("Unable to load", ignoreCase = true) -> {
   436→                    throw S3DownloadException(
   437→                        "AWS credentials not found. Configure via:\n" +
   438→                        "  - Environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)\n" +
   439→                        "  - AWS credentials file (~/.aws/credentials)\n" +
   440→                        "  - IAM role (for EC2/ECS deployments)\n" +
   441→                        "  - Use --aws-profile to specify a named profile"
   442→                    )
   443→                }
   444→                message.contains("network", ignoreCase = true) ||
   445→                message.contains("connect", ignoreCase = true) ||
   446→                message.contains("timeout", ignoreCase = true) -> {
   447→                    throw S3DownloadException(
   448→                        "Network error connecting to S3. Check:\n" +
   449→                        "  - Internet connectivity\n" +
   450→                        "  - AWS region (use --aws-region if bucket is in a different region)\n" +
   451→                        "  - Firewall/proxy settings"
   452→                    )
   453→                }
   454→                else -> throw S3DownloadException("S3 client error: $message")
   455→            }
   456→        } catch (e: S3DownloadException) {
   457→            throw e
   458→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   459→            log.error("Unexpected error listing S3 objects", e)
   460→            throw S3DownloadException("Unexpected error: ${e.message}")
   461→        } finally {
   462→            s3Client.close()
   463→        }
   464→    }
   465→
   466→    /**
   467→     * Clean up a temporary file
   468→     * Call this in a finally block after processing
   469→     */
   470→    fun cleanupTempFile(tempFile: Path?) {
   471→        if (tempFile != null) {
   472→            try {
   473→                if (Files.deleteIfExists(tempFile)) {
   474→                    log.debug("Cleaned up temp file: $tempFile")
   475→                }
   476→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   477→                log.warn("Failed to delete temp file: $tempFile", e)
   478→            }
   479→        }
   480→    }
   481→}
   482→
   483→/**
   484→ * Exception for S3 download errors with user-friendly messages
   485→ */
   486→class S3DownloadException(message: String, cause: Throwable? = null) : RuntimeException(message, cause)
   487→
   488→/**
   489→ * Info about an S3 object returned by listObjects
   490→ */
   491→data class S3ObjectInfo(
   492→    val key: String,
   493→    val size: Long,
   494→    val lastModified: Instant
   495→)
   496→

1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.persistence.*
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Email
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Pattern
     8→import java.time.Instant
     9→
    10→/**
    11→ * UserMapping entity - stores mappings between user emails, AWS account IDs, domains, and IP addresses
    12→ *
    13→ * Features: 013-user-mapping-upload, 020-i-want-to (IP address mapping), 042-future-user-mappings
    14→ * Purpose: Enable role-based access control across multiple AWS accounts, domains, and IP addresses
    15→ * Now supports future user mappings - mappings for users who don't yet exist in the system
    16→ *
    17→ * Business Rules:
    18→ * - Email is REQUIRED
    19→ * - At least one of AWS Account ID, Domain, or IP Address must be provided
    20→ * - One email can map to multiple AWS accounts (many-to-many)
    21→ * - One email can map to multiple domains (many-to-many)
    22→ * - One email can map to multiple IP addresses/ranges (many-to-many)
    23→ * - Unique constraint on (email, awsAccountId, domain, ipAddress) prevents duplicates
    24→ * - Email and domain are normalized to lowercase for case-insensitive matching
    25→ * - AWS account IDs must be exactly 12 numeric digits when provided
    26→ * - IP addresses support three formats: single (192.168.1.100), CIDR (192.168.1.0/24), dash range (192.168.1.1-192.168.1.100)
    27→ * - User reference is nullable - allows future user mappings (Feature 042)
    28→ * - AppliedAt tracks when future user mapping was applied to a user (Feature 042)
    29→ *
    30→ * Example Mappings:
    31→ * - john@example.com → 123456789012 → null → null (email + AWS account only)
    32→ * - john@example.com → null → example.com → null (email + domain only)
    33→ * - john@example.com → null → null → 192.168.1.0/24 (email + IP range)
    34→ * - john@example.com → 123456789012 → example.com → 192.168.1.0/24 (email + AWS + domain + IP)
    35→ * - future@example.com → 123456789012 → null → null + user=null + appliedAt=null (future user mapping)
    36→ *
    37→ * Related to: Feature 013 (User Mapping Upload), Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping), Feature 042 (Future User Mappings)
    38→ */
    39→@Entity
    40→@Table(
    41→    name = "user_mapping",
    42→    uniqueConstraints = [
    43→        UniqueConstraint(
    44→            name = "uk_user_mapping_composite",
    45→            columnNames = ["email", "aws_account_id", "domain", "ip_address"]
    46→        )
    47→    ],
    48→    indexes = [
    49→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_email", columnList = "email"),
    50→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_aws_account", columnList = "aws_account_id"),
    51→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_domain", columnList = "domain"),
    52→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_email_aws", columnList = "email,aws_account_id"),
    53→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_ip_address", columnList = "ip_address"),
    54→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_ip_range", columnList = "ip_range_start,ip_range_end"),
    55→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_email_ip", columnList = "email,ip_address"),
    56→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_applied_at", columnList = "applied_at"), // Feature 042: Efficient filtering for Current vs Applied History tabs
    57→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_status", columnList = "status"), // Feature 049: Status filtering
    58→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_email_status", columnList = "email,status") // Feature 049: User + status queries
    59→    ]
    60→)
    61→@Serdeable
    62→data class UserMapping(
    63→    @Id
    64→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    65→    var id: Long? = null,
    66→
    67→    @Column(nullable = false, length = 255)
    68→    @Email(message = "Invalid email format")
    69→    @NotBlank(message = "Email address is required")
    70→    var email: String,
    71→
    72→    // Feature 042: User reference (nullable for future user mappings)
    73→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY, optional = true)
    74→    @JoinColumn(name = "user_id", nullable = true)
    75→    var user: User? = null,
    76→
    77→    // Feature 042: Timestamp when mapping was applied to user (null = not yet applied)
    78→    @Column(name = "applied_at", nullable = true)
    79→    var appliedAt: Instant? = null,
    80→
    81→    @Column(name = "aws_account_id", nullable = true, length = 12)
    82→    @Pattern(regexp = "^\\d{12}$", message = "AWS Account ID must be exactly 12 numeric digits")
    83→    var awsAccountId: String?,
    84→
    85→    @Column(nullable = true, length = 255)
    86→    @Pattern(regexp = "^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+$", message = "Domain must contain only letters, numbers, dots, and hyphens")
    87→    var domain: String?,
    88→
    89→    // IP Address Mapping Fields (Feature 020)
    90→    @Column(name = "ip_address", nullable = true, length = 100)
    91→    var ipAddress: String? = null,
    92→
    93→    @Column(name = "ip_range_type", nullable = true, length = 20)
    94→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
    95→    var ipRangeType: IpRangeType? = null,
    96→
    97→    @Column(name = "ip_range_start", nullable = true)
    98→    var ipRangeStart: Long? = null,
    99→
   100→    @Column(name = "ip_range_end", nullable = true)
   101→    var ipRangeEnd: Long? = null,
   102→
   103→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
   104→    var createdAt: Instant? = null,
   105→
   106→    @Column(name = "updated_at")
   107→    var updatedAt: Instant? = null,
   108→
   109→    // Feature 049: Status tracking for pending vs active mappings
   110→    @Column(name = "status", nullable = false, length = 20)
   111→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
   112→    var status: MappingStatus = MappingStatus.ACTIVE
   113→) {
   114→    /**
   115→     * Lifecycle callback - executed before entity is persisted to database
   116→     * Normalizes email and domain to lowercase, trims whitespace
   117→     */
   118→    @PrePersist
   119→    fun onCreate() {
   120→        val now = Instant.now()
   121→        createdAt = now
   122→        updatedAt = now
   123→        // Normalize email and domain to lowercase for case-insensitive matching
   124→        email = email.lowercase().trim()
   125→        domain = domain?.lowercase()?.trim()
   126→        awsAccountId = awsAccountId?.trim()
   127→
   128→        // Feature 049: Set status based on user existence
   129→        // PENDING if user is null (future user mapping), ACTIVE otherwise
   130→        if (user == null && status == MappingStatus.ACTIVE) {
   131→            status = MappingStatus.PENDING
   132→        }
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    /**
   136→     * Lifecycle callback - executed before entity is updated in database
   137→     * Updates the updatedAt timestamp
   138→     */
   139→    @PreUpdate
   140→    fun onUpdate() {
   141→        updatedAt = Instant.now()
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    /**
   145→     * Feature 042: Returns true if this is a future user mapping (not yet applied)
   146→     * A future mapping has no user reference and no appliedAt timestamp
   147→     */
   148→    fun isFutureMapping(): Boolean {
   149→        return user == null && appliedAt == null
   150→    }
   151→
   152→    /**
   153→     * Feature 042: Returns true if this is an applied historical mapping
   154→     * An applied mapping has an appliedAt timestamp (regardless of user reference)
   155→     */
   156→    fun isAppliedMapping(): Boolean {
   157→        return appliedAt != null
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    override fun toString(): String {
   161→        return "UserMapping(id=$id, email='$email', awsAccountId='$awsAccountId', domain='$domain', ipAddress='$ipAddress', ipRangeType=$ipRangeType)"
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
   165→        if (this === other) return true
   166→        if (other !is UserMapping) return false
   167→        return id != null && id == other.id
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   171→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   172→    }
   173→}
   174→

1→package com.secman.repository

     3→import com.secman.domain.UserMapping
     4→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query
     5→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Repository
     6→import io.micronaut.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository
     7→import java.util.*
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Repository for UserMapping entity
    11→ * 
    12→ * Feature: 013-user-mapping-upload
    13→ * 
    14→ * Provides CRUD operations and query methods for user-to-AWS-account-to-domain mappings.
    15→ * Used by UserMappingImportService for bulk imports and by future RBAC features
    16→ * for access control lookups.
    17→ * 
    18→ * Related to: Feature 013 (User Mapping Upload)
    19→ */
    20→@Repository
    21→interface UserMappingRepository : JpaRepository<UserMapping, Long> {
    22→
    23→    /**
    24→     * Find all mappings for a specific email address
    25→     * 
    26→     * Use case: Get all AWS accounts and domains a user has access to
    27→     * 
    28→     * @param email User's email address (case-insensitive, will be normalized)
    29→     * @return List of mappings for the email
    30→     */
    31→    fun findByEmail(email: String): List<UserMapping>
    32→
    33→    /**
    34→     * Find all mappings for a specific AWS account
    35→     * 
    36→     * Use case: Get all users with access to an AWS account
    37→     * 
    38→     * @param awsAccountId AWS account identifier (12-digit string)
    39→     * @return List of mappings for the AWS account
    40→     */
    41→    fun findByAwsAccountId(awsAccountId: String): List<UserMapping>
    42→
    43→    /**
    44→     * Find all mappings for a specific domain
    45→     * 
    46→     * Use case: Get all users within an organizational domain
    47→     * 
    48→     * @param domain Organizational domain name
    49→     * @return List of mappings for the domain
    50→     */
    51→    fun findByDomain(domain: String): List<UserMapping>
    52→
    53→    /**
    54→     * Check if a specific mapping exists (duplicate detection)
    55→     *
    56→     * Use case: Skip duplicate mappings during Excel import
    57→     *
    58→     * @param email User's email address (required)
    59→     * @param awsAccountId AWS account identifier (nullable)
    60→     * @param domain Organizational domain name (nullable)
    61→     * @return true if mapping exists, false otherwise
    62→     */
    63→    fun existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
    64→        email: String,
    65→        awsAccountId: String?,
    66→        domain: String?
    67→    ): Boolean
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Find a specific mapping by composite key
    71→     *
    72→     * Use case: Retrieve mapping for update or verification
    73→     *
    74→     * @param email User's email address (required)
    75→     * @param awsAccountId AWS account identifier (nullable)
    76→     * @param domain Organizational domain name (nullable)
    77→     * @return Optional containing the mapping if found
    78→     */
    79→    fun findByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
    80→        email: String,
    81→        awsAccountId: String?,
    82→        domain: String?
    83→    ): Optional<UserMapping>
    84→
    85→    /**
    86→     * Count total mappings for a user
    87→     * 
    88→     * Use case: Display user's total access scope
    89→     * 
    90→     * @param email User's email address
    91→     * @return Number of mappings for the user
    92→     */
    93→    fun countByEmail(email: String): Long
    94→
    95→    /**
    96→     * Count total mappings for an AWS account
    97→     * 
    98→     * Use case: Display how many users have access to an account
    99→     * 
   100→     * @param awsAccountId AWS account identifier
   101→     * @return Number of mappings for the account
   102→     */
   103→    fun countByAwsAccountId(awsAccountId: String): Long
   104→
   105→    /**
   106→     * Find distinct AWS account IDs for a user
   107→     *
   108→     * Use case: Get list of AWS accounts a user can access
   109→     *
   110→     * @param email User's email address
   111→     * @return List of distinct AWS account IDs (excluding null values)
   112→     */
   113→    @Query("SELECT DISTINCT m.awsAccountId FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.awsAccountId IS NOT NULL")
   114→    fun findDistinctAwsAccountIdByEmail(email: String): List<String>
   115→
   116→    @Query("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m.awsAccountId) FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.awsAccountId IS NOT NULL")
   117→    fun countDistinctAwsAccountsByEmail(email: String): Long
   118→
   119→    /**
   120→     * Find distinct domains for a user
   121→     *
   122→     * Use case: Get list of domains a user has access to
   123→     *
   124→     * @param email User's email address
   125→     * @return List of distinct domains (excluding null values)
   126→     */
   127→    @Query("SELECT DISTINCT m.domain FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.domain IS NOT NULL")
   128→    fun findDistinctDomainByEmail(email: String): List<String>
   129→
   130→    @Query("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m.domain) FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.domain IS NOT NULL")
   131→    fun countDistinctDomainsByEmail(email: String): Long
   132→
   133→    @Query("SELECT DISTINCT m.domain FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.domain IS NOT NULL AND m.domain != ''")
   134→    fun findDistinctDomains(): List<String>
   135→
   136→    // IP Address Mapping - Feature 020
   137→
   138→    /**
   139→     * Find all IP address mappings for a specific email
   140→     *
   141→     * Use case: Get all IP ranges a user has access to
   142→     *
   143→     * Related to: Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping)
   144→     *
   145→     * @param email User's email address
   146→     * @return List of mappings with IP addresses (excluding null IP addresses)
   147→     */
   148→    @Query("SELECT m FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.ipAddress IS NOT NULL")
   149→    fun findIpMappingsByEmail(email: String): List<UserMapping>
   150→
   151→    /**
   152→     * Check if a specific IP mapping exists (duplicate detection)
   153→     *
   154→     * Use case: Skip duplicate IP mappings during CSV/Excel import
   155→     *
   156→     * Related to: Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping)
   157→     *
   158→     * @param email User's email address (required)
   159→     * @param ipAddress IP address string (nullable)
   160→     * @param domain Organizational domain name (nullable)
   161→     * @return true if mapping exists, false otherwise
   162→     */
   163→    fun existsByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   164→        email: String,
   165→        ipAddress: String?,
   166→        domain: String?
   167→    ): Boolean
   168→
   169→    /**
   170→     * Find a specific IP mapping by composite key
   171→     *
   172→     * Use case: Retrieve IP mapping for update or verification
   173→     *
   174→     * Related to: Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping)
   175→     *
   176→     * @param email User's email address (required)
   177→     * @param ipAddress IP address string (nullable)
   178→     * @param domain Organizational domain name (nullable)
   179→     * @return Optional containing the mapping if found
   180→     */
   181→    fun findByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   182→        email: String,
   183→        ipAddress: String?,
   184→        domain: String?
   185→    ): Optional<UserMapping>
   186→
   187→    /**
   188→     * Count total IP mappings for a user
   189→     *
   190→     * Use case: Display user's IP-based access scope
   191→     *
   192→     * Related to: Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping)
   193→     *
   194→     * @param email User's email address
   195→     * @return Number of IP mappings for the user
   196→     */
   197→    @Query("SELECT COUNT(m) FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.ipAddress IS NOT NULL")
   198→    fun countIpMappingsByEmail(email: String): Long
   199→
   200→    // Future User Mapping - Feature 042
   201→
   202→    /**
   203→     * Find mapping by email (case-insensitive)
   204→     *
   205→     * Use case: Lookup future user mapping during user creation for automatic application
   206→     *
   207→     * Related to: Feature 042 (Future User Mappings)
   208→     *

   237→

1→# S3 User Mapping Import
     2→
     3→**Feature**: 065-s3-user-mapping-import
     4→**Last Updated**: 2026-02-13
     5→
     6→Import user mappings (domain and AWS account associations) directly from CSV or JSON files stored in AWS S3 buckets via the CLI.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Overview
    11→
    12→The `import-s3` subcommand extends the existing `manage-user-mappings` CLI with S3 support. This enables automated, scheduled imports from centralized S3 buckets without requiring local file copies.
    13→
    14→Key capabilities:
    15→- Download and import user mapping files from any S3 bucket
    16→- Standard AWS credential chain (environment variables, profiles, IAM roles)
    17→- Dry-run mode for validation before committing changes
    18→- 10MB file size limit with pre-download validation
    19→- Cron-friendly exit codes (0=success, 1=partial errors, 2=fatal S3 error, 3=unexpected)
    20→- Automatic temp file cleanup with restricted permissions
    21→
    22→---
    23→
    24→## Prerequisites
    25→
    26→### 1. AWS Account and Permissions
    27→
    28→The IAM user or role needs the following S3 permissions on the target bucket:
    29→
    30→```json
    31→{
    32→  "Version": "2012-10-17",
    33→  "Statement": [
    34→    {
    35→      "Effect": "Allow",
    36→      "Action": [
    37→        "s3:GetObject",
    38→        "s3:HeadObject"
    39→      ],
    40→      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::your-bucket-name/*"
    41→    }
    42→  ]
    43→}
    44→```
    45→
    46→### 2. CLI Setup
    47→
    48→Build the CLI fat JAR:
    49→
    50→```bash
    51→./gradlew :cli:shadowJar
    52→```
    53→
    54→Verify the command is available:
    55→
    56→```bash
    57→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 --help
    58→```
    59→
    60→### 3. Required Tools
    61→
    62→- **Java 21+** - Runtime for CLI JAR
    63→- **AWS CLI** (optional) - For uploading files to S3 and debugging
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→## Configuration
    68→
    69→### Environment Variables
    70→
    71→| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
    72→|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
    73→| `SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL` | Yes* | - | Admin email for audit logging (alternative: `--admin-user` flag) |
    74→| `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Conditional | - | AWS access key (if not using profile/IAM role) |
    75→| `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Conditional | - | AWS secret key (if not using profile/IAM role) |
    76→| `AWS_ENDPOINT_URL` | No | - | Custom S3 endpoint URL for local testing (e.g. `http://localhost:9090` for S3Mock) |
    77→| `AWS_REGION` | No | SDK default | Default AWS region (can be overridden with `--aws-region`) |
    78→
    79→*Either `SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL` env var or `--admin-user` flag is required.
    80→
    81→### AWS Credential Chain
    82→
    83→The command uses the standard AWS SDK credential resolution order:
    84→
    85→1. **Environment variables**: `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` and `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`
    86→2. **AWS credentials file**: `~/.aws/credentials` (use `--aws-profile` to select a named profile)
    87→3. **IAM role**: Automatic on EC2/ECS/Lambda (no configuration needed)
    88→4. **SSO credentials**: Via `aws sso login`
    89→
    90→---
    91→
    92→## Listing Bucket Contents
    93→
    94→Before importing, you can browse an S3 bucket to discover available files using the `list-bucket` subcommand.
    95→
    96→### List All Objects
    97→
    98→```bash
    99→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings list-bucket \
   100→  --bucket my-company-mappings
   101→```
   102→
   103→### Filter by Prefix
   104→
   105→```bash
   106→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings list-bucket \
   107→  --bucket my-company-mappings \
   108→  --prefix user-mappings/
   109→```
   110→
   111→### With AWS Profile and Region
   112→
   113→```bash
   114→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings list-bucket \
   115→  --bucket my-company-mappings \
   116→  --prefix user-mappings/ \
   117→  --aws-profile production \
   118→  --aws-region eu-west-1
   119→```
   120→
   121→### Example Output
   122→
   123→```
   124→============================================================
   125→List S3 Bucket Contents
   126→============================================================
   127→
   128→Bucket: my-company-mappings
   129→Prefix: user-mappings/
   130→
   131→Listing objects...
   132→Key                                    Size     Last Modified
   133→---------------------------------------------------------------
   134→user-mappings/latest.csv               2.3 KB   2026-02-13 10:30:00
   135→user-mappings/2026-01-backup.csv       1.8 KB   2026-01-15 08:00:00
   136→user-mappings/teams.json               4.1 KB   2026-02-10 14:22:00
   137→
   138→Total: 3 object(s)
   139→```
   140→
   141→### list-bucket Options
   142→
   143→| Option | Short | Required | Default | Description |
   144→|--------|-------|----------|---------|-------------|
   145→| `--bucket` | `-b` | Yes | - | S3 bucket name |
   146→| `--prefix` | `-p` | No | - | Filter objects by key prefix |
   147→| `--aws-region` | - | No | SDK default | AWS region |
   148→| `--aws-profile` | - | No | default chain | AWS credential profile name |
   149→| `--endpoint-url` | - | No | `$AWS_ENDPOINT_URL` | Custom S3 endpoint URL for local testing |
   150→| `--admin-user` | `-u` | No* | `$SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL` | Admin email (inherited from parent) |
   151→
   152→### Required IAM Permissions
   153→
   154→The `list-bucket` command requires the `s3:ListBucket` permission on the target bucket:
   155→
   156→```json
   157→{
   158→  "Effect": "Allow",
   159→  "Action": "s3:ListBucket",
   160→  "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::your-bucket-name"
   161→}
   162→```
   163→
   164→---
   165→
   166→## Usage Examples
   167→
   168→### Basic Import
   169→
   170→```bash
   171→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 \
   172→  --bucket my-company-mappings \
   173→  --key user-mappings/latest.csv
   174→```
   175→
   176→### Dry-Run (Validation Only)
   177→
   178→```bash
   179→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 \
   180→  --bucket my-company-mappings \
   181→  --key user-mappings/latest.csv \
   182→  --dry-run
   183→```
   184→
   185→### With AWS Profile
   186→
   187→```bash
   188→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 \
   189→  --bucket my-company-mappings \
   190→  --key user-mappings/latest.csv \
   191→  --aws-profile production
   192→```
   193→
   194→### With Explicit Region
   195→
   196→```bash
   197→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 \
   198→  --bucket my-company-mappings \
   199→  --key user-mappings/latest.csv \
   200→  --aws-region eu-west-1
   201→```
   202→
   203→### JSON Format
   204→
   205→```bash
   206→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 \
   207→  --bucket my-company-mappings \
   208→  --key user-mappings/latest.json \
   209→  --format JSON
   210→```
   211→
   212→### Cron Job Setup
   213→
   214→```bash
   215→# /etc/cron.d/secman-import
   216→# Run daily at 2 AM, log output, alert on failure
   217→0 2 * * * secman-user /opt/secman/bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 \
   218→  --bucket my-company-mappings \
   219→  --key user-mappings/latest.csv \
   220→  >> /var/log/secman/s3-import.log 2>&1 \
   221→  || echo "S3 import failed with exit code $?" | mail -s "secman S3 import failure" ops@company.com
   222→```
   223→
   224→### Command-Line Options
   225→
   226→| Option | Short | Required | Default | Description |
   227→|--------|-------|----------|---------|-------------|
   228→| `--bucket` | `-b` | Yes | - | S3 bucket name |
   229→| `--key` | `-k` | Yes | - | S3 object key (path to file) |
   230→| `--aws-region` | - | No | SDK default | AWS region |
   231→| `--aws-profile` | - | No | default chain | AWS credential profile name |
   232→| `--format` | - | No | `AUTO` | File format: `CSV`, `JSON`, or `AUTO` |
   233→| `--dry-run` | - | No | `false` | Validate without creating mappings |
   234→| `--endpoint-url` | - | No | `$AWS_ENDPOINT_URL` | Custom S3 endpoint URL for local testing |
   235→| `--admin-user` | `-u` | No* | `$SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL` | Admin email for audit |
   236→
   237→---
   238→
   239→## File Formats
   240→
   241→### CSV Format
   242→
   243→Headers: `email`, `type`, `value` (case-insensitive)
   244→
   245→```csv
   246→email,type,value
   247→alice@company.com,DOMAIN,corp.local
   248→alice@company.com,DOMAIN,prod.company.com
   249→alice@company.com,AWS_ACCOUNT,123456789012
   250→bob@company.com,DOMAIN,dev.company.com
   251→bob@company.com,AWS_ACCOUNT,987654321098
   252→```
   253→
   254→- **type** must be `DOMAIN` or `AWS_ACCOUNT`
   255→- **AWS_ACCOUNT** values must be exactly 12 digits
   256→- Empty lines are skipped
   257→- Maximum 100,000 records per file
   258→
   259→### JSON Format
   260→
   261→```json
   262→[
   263→  {
   264→    "email": "alice@company.com",
   265→    "domains": ["corp.local", "prod.company.com"],
   266→    "awsAccounts": ["123456789012"]
   267→  },
   268→  {
   269→    "email": "bob@company.com",
   270→    "domains": ["dev.company.com"],
   271→    "awsAccounts": ["987654321098"]
   272→  }
   273→]
   274→```
   275→
   276→- Each entry can have `domains`, `awsAccounts`, or both
   277→- Maximum 100,000 entries per file
   278→
   279→---
   280→
   281→## E2E Testing
   282→
   283→An end-to-end test script validates the complete import flow against a real S3 bucket and running backend.
   284→
   285→### Running the Test
   286→
   287→```bash
   288→./tests/s3-user-mapping-import-e2e-test.sh
   289→```
   290→
   291→### With Debug Output
   292→
   293→```bash
   294→DEBUG=1 ./tests/s3-user-mapping-import-e2e-test.sh
   295→```
   296→
   297→### Test Environment Variables
   298→
   299→| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
   300→|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
   301→| `S3_TEST_BUCKET` | Yes | 1Password URI | S3 bucket for test files |
   302→| `S3_TEST_REGION` | Yes | 1Password URI | AWS region for test bucket |
   303→| `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | 1Password URI | AWS credentials |
   304→| `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | 1Password URI | AWS credentials |
   305→| `SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL` | Yes | 1Password URI | Admin email for CLI commands |
   306→| `SECMAN_BASE_URL` | No | `http://localhost:8080` | Backend URL |
   307→
   308→### Expected Output
   309→
   310→```
   311→=== E2E Test: S3 User Mapping Import (065-s3-user-mapping-import) ===
   312→
   313→[PASS] Prerequisites check passed
   314→[PASS] Credentials resolved
   315→[PASS] Step 1: CLI JAR built successfully
   316→[PASS] Step 2: Test CSV uploaded to s3://bucket/e2e-test/user-mappings-1234567890.csv
   317→[PASS] Step 3: Dry-run completed successfully (exit code 0)
   318→[PASS] Step 4: Import completed successfully (exit code 0)
   319→[PASS] Step 5a: Domain mapping for e2e-1234567890.test.local found
   320→[PASS] Step 5b: AWS account mapping for 999900567890 found
   321→[INFO] Step 6: Cleaning up test data...
   322→[PASS] Cleanup completed
   323→
   324→============================================
   325→  S3 USER MAPPING IMPORT E2E TEST PASSED
   326→============================================
   327→```
   328→
   329→### Test Flow
   330→
   331→| Step | Action | Verification |
   332→|------|--------|--------------|
   333→| 1 | Build CLI fat JAR | JAR file exists |
   334→| 2 | Upload test CSV to S3 | `aws s3 cp` succeeds |
   335→| 3 | Dry-run import | Exit code 0, validation messages present |
   336→| 4 | Actual import | Exit code 0, success messages present |
   337→| 5 | List mappings | Test domain and AWS account found |
   338→| 6 | Cleanup | Mappings deleted, S3 file removed |
   339→
   340→---
   341→
   342→## Troubleshooting
   343→
   344→### "AWS credentials not found"
   345→
   346→Configure credentials via one of:
   347→
   348→```bash
   349→# Option 1: Environment variables
   350→export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA...
   351→export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
   352→
   353→# Option 2: AWS CLI configure
   354→aws configure
   355→
   356→# Option 3: Named profile
   357→aws configure --profile myprofile
   358→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 --aws-profile myprofile ...
   359→```
   360→
   361→### "Access denied" (403)
   362→
   363→1. Verify IAM permissions include `s3:GetObject` and `s3:HeadObject`
   364→2. Check bucket policy allows access from your account/IP
   365→3. Ensure the bucket name and key are correct
   366→
   367→### "Bucket does not exist"
   368→
   369→1. Verify bucket name spelling (case-sensitive, lowercase only)
   370→2. Ensure the bucket is in the expected region (use `--aws-region`)
   371→3. Check that the bucket hasn't been deleted
   372→
   373→### "File not found" (NoSuchKey)
   374→
   375→1. Verify the S3 key (path) is correct
   376→2. List objects to confirm: `aws s3 ls s3://bucket/path/`
   377→3. Keys are case-sensitive
   378→
   379→### "File exceeds 10MB limit"
   380→
   381→Split the file into smaller batches. The 10MB limit is checked before download to avoid wasting bandwidth.
   382→
   383→### "File exceeds maximum of 100,000 records"
   384→
   385→Split the file into multiple files with fewer records each. Import them sequentially.
   386→
   387→### "Network error connecting to S3"
   388→
   389→1. Check internet connectivity
   390→2. Verify the region matches the bucket's actual region
   391→3. Check firewall/proxy settings
   392→
   393→### Exit Codes
   394→
   395→| Code | Meaning | Action |
   396→|------|---------|--------|
   397→| 0 | Success | All mappings imported |
   398→| 1 | Partial success | Some mappings had errors, check output |
   399→| 2 | Fatal S3/config error | Fix credentials, bucket, or key |
   400→| 3 | Unexpected error | Check logs, enable debug logging |
   401→
   402→---
   403→
   404→## Security Considerations
   405→
   406→- **Temp files**: Downloaded files are stored in the system temp directory with owner-only permissions (`rw-------`) and are automatically deleted after processing, even on error
   407→- **Credential management**: Uses the standard AWS SDK credential chain; never pass credentials as CLI arguments
   408→- **File size limit**: 10MB pre-download check prevents large file abuse
   409→- **Record count limit**: Maximum 100,000 records per file prevents memory exhaustion
   410→- **Stack traces**: Internal error details are only logged at debug level, not exposed to stdout/stderr
   411→- **Audit logging**: All import operations are logged with admin email, operation type, and mapping details
   412→- **Input validation**: Bucket names, object keys, email addresses, domains, and AWS account IDs are validated before processing
   413→
   414→---
   415→
   416→## Testing with S3Mock
   417→
   418→You can use [Adobe S3Mock](https://github.com/adobe/S3Mock) (or MinIO, LocalStack) to test S3 imports locally without an AWS account.
   419→
   420→### Setup
   421→
   422→```bash
   423→# Start S3Mock
   424→docker run -p 9090:9090 adobe/s3mock
   425→
   426→# Create a test bucket and upload a mapping file
   427→aws s3api create-bucket --bucket test --endpoint-url http://localhost:9090
   428→aws s3api put-object --bucket test --key mappings.csv --body ./test-mappings.csv --endpoint-url http://localhost:9090
   429→```
   430→
   431→### Using --endpoint-url
   432→
   433→```bash
   434→# List bucket contents
   435→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings list-bucket \
   436→  --bucket test \
   437→  --endpoint-url http://localhost:9090
   438→
   439→# Dry-run import
   440→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 \
   441→  --bucket test \
   442→  --key mappings.csv \
   443→  --endpoint-url http://localhost:9090 \
   444→  --dry-run
   445→```
   446→
   447→### Using AWS_ENDPOINT_URL Environment Variable
   448→
   449→```bash
   450→export AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=http://localhost:9090
   451→
   452→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings list-bucket --bucket test
   453→./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 --bucket test --key mappings.csv --dry-run
   454→```
   455→
   456→When `--endpoint-url` is set (or `AWS_ENDPOINT_URL`), the CLI automatically enables path-style S3 access, which is required by local S3 simulators.
   457→
   458→---
