1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.User
     4→import com.secman.domain.UserMapping
     5→import com.secman.dto.CreateUserMappingRequest
     6→import com.secman.dto.UpdateUserMappingRequest
     7→import com.secman.dto.UserMappingResponse
     8→import com.secman.dto.toResponse
     9→import com.secman.event.UserCreatedEvent
    10→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    11→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
    13→import io.micronaut.runtime.event.annotation.EventListener
    14→import io.micronaut.scheduling.annotation.Async
    15→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    16→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    17→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    18→import java.time.Instant
    19→
    20→@Singleton
    21→open class UserMappingService(
    22→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    23→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository,
    24→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    25→    private val ipAddressParser: IpAddressParser
    26→) {
    27→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(UserMappingService::class.java)
    28→    
    29→    fun getUserMappings(userId: Long): List<UserMappingResponse> {
    30→        val user = userRepository.findById(userId)
    31→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("User not found") }
    32→
    33→        val mappings = userMappingRepository.findByEmail(user.email)
    34→        return mappings.map { it.toResponse() }
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    fun getDistinctDomains(): List<String> {
    38→        val mappingDomains = userMappingRepository.findDistinctDomains()
    39→        val assetDomains = assetRepository.findDistinctAdDomains()
    40→        return (mappingDomains + assetDomains)
    41→            .map { it.lowercase() }
    42→            .distinct()
    43→            .sorted()
    44→    }
    45→    
    46→    @Transactional
    47→    open fun createMapping(userId: Long, request: CreateUserMappingRequest): UserMappingResponse {
    48→        // Validate at least one field (Feature 020: extended to include ipAddress)
    49→        if (request.awsAccountId == null && request.domain == null && request.ipAddress == null) {
    50→            throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one of Domain, AWS Account ID, or IP Address must be provided")
    51→        }
    52→
    53→        // Get user email
    54→        val user = userRepository.findById(userId)
    55→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("User not found") }
    56→
    57→        // Parse IP address if provided (Feature 020)
    58→        var ipParseResult: IpAddressParser.IpParseResult? = null
    59→        if (request.ipAddress != null) {
    60→            try {
    61→                ipParseResult = ipAddressParser.parse(request.ipAddress)
    62→            } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    63→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid IP address format: ${e.message}", e)
    64→            }
    65→        }
    66→
    67→        // Check for duplicates (extended for IP addresses - Feature 020)
    68→        if (request.ipAddress != null) {
    69→            if (userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
    70→                    user.email, request.ipAddress, request.domain
    71→                )) {
    72→                throw IllegalStateException("IP mapping already exists for this email, IP address, and domain")
    73→            }
    74→        } else {
    75→            if (userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
    76→                    user.email, request.awsAccountId, request.domain
    77→                )) {
    78→                throw IllegalStateException("AWS mapping already exists for this email, AWS account, and domain")
    79→            }
    80→        }
    81→
    82→        // Create and save
    83→        val mapping = UserMapping(
    84→            email = user.email,
    85→            awsAccountId = request.awsAccountId,
    86→            domain = request.domain
    87→        )
    88→
    89→        // Set IP fields if IP address was provided (Feature 020)
    90→        if (ipParseResult != null) {
    91→            mapping.ipAddress = request.ipAddress
    92→            mapping.ipRangeType = ipParseResult.rangeType
    93→            mapping.ipRangeStart = ipParseResult.startIpNumeric
    94→            mapping.ipRangeEnd = ipParseResult.endIpNumeric
    95→        }
    96→
    97→        val savedMapping = userMappingRepository.save(mapping)
    98→        return savedMapping.toResponse()
    99→    }
   100→    
   101→    @Transactional
   102→    open fun updateMapping(userId: Long, mappingId: Long, request: UpdateUserMappingRequest): UserMappingResponse {
   103→        // Validate at least one field (Feature 020: extended to include ipAddress)
   104→        if (request.awsAccountId == null && request.domain == null && request.ipAddress == null) {
   105→            throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one of Domain, AWS Account ID, or IP Address must be provided")
   106→        }
   107→
   108→        // Get user
   109→        val user = userRepository.findById(userId)
   110→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("User not found") }
   111→
   112→        // Get mapping and verify ownership
   113→        val mapping = userMappingRepository.findById(mappingId)
   114→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Mapping not found") }
   115→
   116→        if (mapping.email != user.email) {
   117→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Mapping does not belong to user")
   118→        }
   119→
   120→        // Parse IP address if provided (Feature 020)
   121→        var ipParseResult: IpAddressParser.IpParseResult? = null
   122→        if (request.ipAddress != null) {
   123→            try {
   124→                ipParseResult = ipAddressParser.parse(request.ipAddress)
   125→            } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   126→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid IP address format: ${e.message}", e)
   127→            }
   128→        }
   129→
   130→        // Check for duplicates (excluding current mapping) - Feature 020
   131→        if (request.ipAddress != null) {
   132→            val isDuplicate = userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   133→                user.email, request.ipAddress, request.domain
   134→            ) && (mapping.ipAddress != request.ipAddress || mapping.domain != request.domain)
   135→
   136→            if (isDuplicate) {
   137→                throw IllegalStateException("IP mapping already exists for this email, IP address, and domain")
   138→            }
   139→        } else {
   140→            val isDuplicate = userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   141→                user.email, request.awsAccountId, request.domain
   142→            ) && (mapping.awsAccountId != request.awsAccountId || mapping.domain != request.domain)
   143→
   144→            if (isDuplicate) {
   145→                throw IllegalStateException("AWS mapping already exists for this email, AWS account, and domain")
   146→            }
   147→        }
   148→
   149→        // Update
   150→        mapping.awsAccountId = request.awsAccountId
   151→        mapping.domain = request.domain
   152→
   153→        // Update IP fields if IP address was provided (Feature 020)
   154→        if (ipParseResult != null) {
   155→            mapping.ipAddress = request.ipAddress
   156→            mapping.ipRangeType = ipParseResult.rangeType
   157→            mapping.ipRangeStart = ipParseResult.startIpNumeric
   158→            mapping.ipRangeEnd = ipParseResult.endIpNumeric
   159→        } else {
   160→            // Clear IP fields if no IP address provided
   161→            mapping.ipAddress = null
   162→            mapping.ipRangeType = null
   163→            mapping.ipRangeStart = null
   164→            mapping.ipRangeEnd = null
   165→        }
   166→
   167→        val updated = userMappingRepository.update(mapping)
   168→        return updated.toResponse()
   169→    }
   170→    
   171→    @Transactional
   172→    open fun deleteMapping(userId: Long, mappingId: Long): Boolean {
   173→        val user = userRepository.findById(userId)
   174→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("User not found") }
   175→
   176→        val mapping = userMappingRepository.findById(mappingId)
   177→            .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Mapping not found") }
   178→
   179→        if (mapping.email != user.email) {
   180→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Mapping does not belong to user")
   181→        }
   182→
   183→        userMappingRepository.delete(mapping)
   184→        return true
   185→    }
   186→
   187→    // Feature 042: Future User Mapping Support
   188→
   189→    /**
   190→     * Event listener for user creation - automatically applies future user mappings
   191→     *
   192→     * When a new user is created (via OAuth auto-provisioning or manual creation),
   193→     * this method checks for existing future user mappings (email-only mappings)
   194→     * and applies them to the new user account.
   195→     *
   196→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   197→     *
   198→     * @param event UserCreatedEvent containing the newly created user
   199→     */
   200→    @EventListener
   201→    @Async
   202→    open fun onUserCreated(event: UserCreatedEvent) {
   203→        log.info("User created event received: email=${event.user.email}, source=${event.source}")
   204→
   205→        try {
   206→            applyFutureUserMapping(event.user)
   207→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   208→            log.error("Failed to apply future user mapping for user ${event.user.email}", e)
   209→            // Don't throw - user creation should not fail if mapping application fails
   210→        }
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    /**
   214→     * Apply future user mapping to a newly created user
   215→     *
   216→     * Looks up any existing future user mappings (mappings with matching email but no user reference)
   217→     * and applies them to the new user. Uses "pre-existing mapping wins" strategy for conflicts.
   218→     *
   219→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   220→     *
   221→     * @param user The newly created user
   222→     * @return Number of mappings applied
   223→     */
   224→    @Transactional
   225→    open fun applyFutureUserMapping(user: User): Int {
   226→        var appliedCount = 0
   227→
   228→        // Find future user mappings (case-insensitive email match, no user reference, not yet applied)
   229→        val futureMappings = userMappingRepository.findByEmail(user.email)
   230→            .filter { it.user == null && it.appliedAt == null }
   231→
   232→        if (futureMappings.isEmpty()) {
   233→            log.debug("No future user mappings found for email: ${user.email}")
   234→            return 0
   235→        }
   236→
   237→        log.info("Found ${futureMappings.size} future user mapping(s) for email: ${user.email}")
   238→
   239→        for (futureMapping in futureMappings) {
   240→            // Check for conflicting pre-existing mapping (mapping with user reference for same composite key)
   241→            val hasConflict = if (futureMapping.ipAddress != null) {
   242→                userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   243→                    user.email, futureMapping.ipAddress, futureMapping.domain
   244→                ) && userMappingRepository.findByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   245→                    user.email, futureMapping.ipAddress, futureMapping.domain
   246→                ).map { it.user != null }.orElse(false)
   247→            } else {
   248→                userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   249→                    user.email, futureMapping.awsAccountId, futureMapping.domain
   250→                ) && userMappingRepository.findByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   251→                    user.email, futureMapping.awsAccountId, futureMapping.domain
   252→                ).map { it.user != null }.orElse(false)
   253→            }
   254→
   255→            if (hasConflict) {
   256→                log.warn("Pre-existing mapping conflicts with future mapping id=${futureMapping.id}, skipping application")
   257→                // Mark as applied but don't link to user (conflict resolution strategy)
   258→                futureMapping.appliedAt = Instant.now()
   259→                userMappingRepository.update(futureMapping)
   260→                continue
   261→            }
   262→
   263→            // Apply the future mapping to the user
   264→            futureMapping.user = user
   265→            futureMapping.appliedAt = Instant.now()
   266→            userMappingRepository.update(futureMapping)
   267→
   268→            log.info("Applied future user mapping id=${futureMapping.id} to user ${user.email}")
   269→            appliedCount++
   270→        }
   271→
   272→        log.info("Applied $appliedCount future user mapping(s) to user ${user.email}")
   273→        return appliedCount
   274→    }
   275→
   276→    /**
   277→     * Get current mappings (future + active) with pagination
   278→     *
   279→     * Returns all mappings that have not yet been applied (appliedAt IS NULL).
   280→     * This includes both future user mappings and active user mappings.
   281→     *
   282→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   283→     *
   284→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters (page, size, sort)
   285→     * @return Page of current mappings
   286→     */
   287→    fun getCurrentMappings(pageable: io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable): io.micronaut.data.model.Page<UserMappingResponse> {
   288→        val page = userMappingRepository.findByAppliedAtIsNull(pageable)
   289→        return page.map { it.toResponse() }
   290→    }
   291→
   292→    /**
   293→     * Get applied historical mappings with pagination
   294→     *
   295→     * Returns all mappings that have been applied to users (appliedAt IS NOT NULL).
   296→     *
   297→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   298→     *
   299→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters (page, size, sort)
   300→     * @return Page of applied historical mappings
   301→     */
   302→    fun getAppliedHistory(pageable: io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable): io.micronaut.data.model.Page<UserMappingResponse> {
   303→        val page = userMappingRepository.findByAppliedAtIsNotNull(pageable)
   304→        return page.map { it.toResponse() }
   305→    }
   306→
   307→    /**
   308→     * Count current mappings (future + active)
   309→     *
   310→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   311→     *
   312→     * @return Number of current mappings (appliedAt IS NULL)
   313→     */
   314→    fun countCurrentMappings(): Long {
   315→        return userMappingRepository.countByAppliedAtIsNull()
   316→    }
   317→
   318→    /**
   319→     * Count applied historical mappings
   320→     *
   321→     * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
   322→     *
   323→     * @return Number of applied historical mappings (appliedAt IS NOT NULL)
   324→     */
   325→    fun countAppliedHistory(): Long {
   326→        return userMappingRepository.countByAppliedAtIsNotNull()
   327→    }
   328→}

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.UserMapping
     4→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
     5→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     6→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     7→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
     8→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.*
     9→import org.apache.poi.xssf.usermodel.XSSFWorkbook
    10→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    11→import java.io.InputStream
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Service for importing user mappings from Excel files
    15→ *
    16→ * Features: 013-user-mapping-upload, 042-future-user-mappings
    17→ *
    18→ * Responsibilities:
    19→ * - Parse Excel files (.xlsx format)
    20→ * - Validate headers (case-insensitive matching)
    21→ * - Validate each row (email + at least one of AWS account ID or domain)
    22→ * - Skip invalid rows, continue processing valid rows
    23→ * - Detect and skip duplicate mappings
    24→ * - Return detailed import results
    25→ * - Support future user mappings (mappings for users who don't exist yet)
    26→ *
    27→ * Excel Format:
    28→ * - Column 1: Email Address (required, must contain @)
    29→ * - Column 2: AWS Account ID (optional, exactly 12 numeric digits when provided)
    30→ * - Column 3: Domain (optional, alphanumeric + dots + hyphens when provided)
    31→ * - At least one of AWS Account ID or Domain must be provided
    32→ *
    33→ * Feature 042: Future User Mappings
    34→ * - Imported mappings do NOT require users to exist in the system
    35→ * - When users are later created (manual or OAuth), mappings are automatically applied
    36→ * - All imported mappings have user=null and appliedAt=null (future mapping state)
    37→ *
    38→ * Related to: Feature 013 (User Mapping Upload), Feature 042 (Future User Mappings)
    39→ */
    40→@Singleton
    41→open class UserMappingImportService(
    42→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository
    43→) {
    44→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(UserMappingImportService::class.java)
    45→    
    46→    companion object {
    47→        private val REQUIRED_HEADERS = listOf(
    48→            "Email Address", "AWS Account ID", "Domain"
    49→        )
    50→        private const val AWS_ACCOUNT_ID_PATTERN = "^\\d{12}$"
    51→        private const val DOMAIN_PATTERN = "^[a-z0-9.-]+$"
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    /**
    55→     * Data class for import results
    56→     */
    57→    @Serdeable
    58→    data class ImportResult(
    59→        val message: String,
    60→        val imported: Int,
    61→        val skipped: Int,
    62→        val errors: List<String> = emptyList()
    63→    )
    64→
    65→    /**
    66→     * Import user mappings from Excel file
    67→     * 
    68→     * @param inputStream Excel file input stream (.xlsx format)
    69→     * @return ImportResult with counts and error details
    70→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if file format is invalid
    71→     */
    72→    @Transactional
    73→    open fun importFromExcel(inputStream: InputStream): ImportResult {
    74→        val workbook = XSSFWorkbook(inputStream)
    75→        val sheet = workbook.getSheetAt(0) 
    76→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Excel file has no sheets")
    77→
    78→        // Validate headers
    79→        val headerValidation = validateHeaders(sheet)
    80→        if (headerValidation != null) {
    81→            workbook.close()
    82→            throw IllegalArgumentException(headerValidation)
    83→        }
    84→
    85→        val headerMap = getHeaderMapping(sheet)
    86→        val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
    87→        var imported = 0
    88→        var skipped = 0
    89→
    90→        // Process rows (skip header row 0)
    91→        for (rowIndex in 1..sheet.lastRowNum) {
    92→            val row = sheet.getRow(rowIndex) ?: continue
    93→            
    94→            // Skip completely empty rows
    95→            if (isRowEmpty(row)) {
    96→                continue
    97→            }
    98→            
    99→            try {
   100→                val mapping = parseRowToUserMapping(row, headerMap, rowIndex + 1)
   101→                if (mapping != null) {
   102→                    // Check for duplicate
   103→                    if (userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   104→                        mapping.email, mapping.awsAccountId, mapping.domain
   105→                    )) {
   106→                        skipped++
   107→                        log.debug("Skipped duplicate mapping at row {}: {}", rowIndex + 1, mapping)
   108→                    } else {
   109→                        userMappingRepository.save(mapping)
   110→                        imported++
   111→                        log.debug("Imported mapping at row {}: {}", rowIndex + 1, mapping)
   112→                    }
   113→                }
   114→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   115→                skipped++
   116→                val errorMsg = "Row ${rowIndex + 1}: ${e.message}"
   117→                errors.add(errorMsg)
   118→                log.warn("Failed to parse row {}: {}", rowIndex + 1, e.message)
   119→            }
   120→        }
   121→
   122→        workbook.close()
   123→
   124→        val message = if (imported > 0 || skipped > 0) {
   125→            "Imported $imported mappings, skipped $skipped"
   126→        } else {
   127→            "No data rows found in file"
   128→        }
   129→
   130→        return ImportResult(
   131→            message = message,
   132→            imported = imported,
   133→            skipped = skipped,
   134→            errors = errors.take(20) // Limit error list to first 20
   135→        )
   136→    }
   137→
   138→    /**
   139→     * Validate that all required headers are present
   140→     */
   141→    private fun validateHeaders(sheet: Sheet): String? {
   142→        val headerRow = sheet.getRow(0) ?: return "Header row not found"
   143→        
   144→        val actualHeaders = mutableListOf<String>()
   145→        for (cellIndex in 0 until headerRow.lastCellNum) {
   146→            val cell = headerRow.getCell(cellIndex)
   147→            if (cell != null) {
   148→                actualHeaders.add(getCellValueAsString(cell).trim())
   149→            }
   150→        }
   151→
   152→        val missingHeaders = REQUIRED_HEADERS.filter { required ->
   153→            actualHeaders.none { it.equals(required, ignoreCase = true) }
   154→        }
   155→
   156→        if (missingHeaders.isNotEmpty()) {
   157→            return "Missing required columns: ${missingHeaders.joinToString(", ")}"
   158→        }
   159→
   160→        return null
   161→    }
   162→
   163→    /**
   164→     * Create a mapping from header names to column indices
   165→     */
   166→    private fun getHeaderMapping(sheet: Sheet): Map<String, Int> {
   167→        val headerRow = sheet.getRow(0)
   168→        val headerMap = mutableMapOf<String, Int>()
   169→
   170→        for (cellIndex in 0 until headerRow.lastCellNum) {
   171→            val cell = headerRow.getCell(cellIndex)
   172→            if (cell != null) {
   173→                val headerName = getCellValueAsString(cell).trim()
   174→                REQUIRED_HEADERS.forEach { required ->
   175→                    if (headerName.equals(required, ignoreCase = true)) {
   176→                        headerMap[required] = cellIndex
   177→                    }
   178→                }
   179→            }
   180→        }
   181→
   182→        return headerMap
   183→    }
   184→
   185→    /**
   186→     * Parse a single row into a UserMapping
   187→     *
   188→     * @param row Excel row to parse
   189→     * @param headerMap Mapping of header names to column indices
   190→     * @param rowNumber Row number for error messages (1-based)
   191→     * @return UserMapping if valid, null if row should be skipped
   192→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if validation fails
   193→     */
   194→    private fun parseRowToUserMapping(row: Row, headerMap: Map<String, Int>, rowNumber: Int): UserMapping? {
   195→        val email = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "Email Address")?.trim()
   196→        val awsAccountId = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "AWS Account ID")?.trim()
   197→        val domain = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "Domain")?.trim()
   198→
   199→        // Validate required fields
   200→        if (email.isNullOrBlank()) {
   201→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Email address is required")
   202→        }
   203→
   204→        // At least one of AWS Account ID or Domain must be provided
   205→        if (awsAccountId.isNullOrBlank() && domain.isNullOrBlank()) {
   206→            throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one of AWS Account ID or Domain must be provided")
   207→        }
   208→
   209→        // Validate email format
   210→        if (!validateEmail(email)) {
   211→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid email format: $email")
   212→        }
   213→
   214→        // Validate AWS account ID format (12 digits) - only if provided
   215→        if (!awsAccountId.isNullOrBlank() && !validateAwsAccountId(awsAccountId)) {
   216→            throw IllegalArgumentException("AWS Account ID must be exactly 12 numeric digits: $awsAccountId")
   217→        }
   218→
   219→        // Validate domain format - only if provided
   220→        if (!domain.isNullOrBlank() && !validateDomain(domain)) {
   221→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid domain format: $domain")
   222→        }
   223→
   224→        return UserMapping(
   225→            email = email.lowercase(),
   226→            awsAccountId = if (awsAccountId.isNullOrBlank()) null else awsAccountId,
   227→            domain = if (domain.isNullOrBlank()) null else domain.lowercase()
   228→        )
   229→    }
   230→
   231→    /**
   232→     * Validate email address format
   233→     */
   234→    private fun validateEmail(email: String): Boolean {
   235→        return email.contains("@") && 
   236→               email.length >= 3 && 
   237→               email.length <= 255 &&
   238→               email.indexOf("@") > 0 &&
   239→               email.indexOf("@") < email.length - 1
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    /**
   243→     * Validate AWS account ID format (exactly 12 numeric digits)
   244→     */
   245→    private fun validateAwsAccountId(accountId: String): Boolean {
   246→        return accountId.matches(Regex(AWS_ACCOUNT_ID_PATTERN))
   247→    }
   248→
   249→    /**
   250→     * Validate domain format
   251→     */
   252→    private fun validateDomain(domain: String): Boolean {
   253→        val normalized = domain.lowercase()
   254→        return normalized.matches(Regex(DOMAIN_PATTERN)) &&
   255→               !normalized.startsWith(".") &&
   256→               !normalized.endsWith(".") &&
   257→               !normalized.startsWith("-") &&
   258→               !normalized.endsWith("-") &&
   259→               !normalized.contains(" ")
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    /**
   263→     * Get cell value from row using header mapping
   264→     */
   265→    private fun getCellValue(row: Row, headerMap: Map<String, Int>, headerName: String): String? {
   266→        val cellIndex = headerMap[headerName] ?: return null
   267→        val cell = row.getCell(cellIndex) ?: return null
   268→        return getCellValueAsString(cell)
   269→    }
   270→
   271→    /**
   272→     * Convert cell value to string, handling different cell types
   273→     * CRITICAL: Use DataFormatter to preserve AWS account ID precision
   274→     */
   275→    private fun getCellValueAsString(cell: Cell): String {
   276→        return when (cell.cellType) {
   277→            CellType.STRING -> cell.stringCellValue
   278→            CellType.NUMERIC -> {
   279→                // Use DataFormatter to preserve precision
   280→                // Without this, "123456789012" becomes "1.23E+11"
   281→                val formatter = DataFormatter()
   282→                formatter.formatCellValue(cell)
   283→            }
   284→            CellType.BOOLEAN -> cell.booleanCellValue.toString()
   285→            CellType.FORMULA -> {
   286→                try {
   287→                    when (cell.cachedFormulaResultType) {
   288→                        CellType.STRING -> cell.richStringCellValue.string
   289→                        CellType.NUMERIC -> {
   290→                            val formatter = DataFormatter()
   291→                            formatter.formatCellValue(cell)
   292→                        }
   293→                        CellType.BOOLEAN -> cell.booleanCellValue.toString()
   294→                        else -> ""
   295→                    }
   296→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   297→                    log.warn("Failed to read cached formula result: {}", e.message)
   298→                    ""
   299→                }
   300→            }
   301→            CellType.BLANK -> ""
   302→            else -> ""
   303→        }
   304→    }
   305→
   306→    /**
   307→     * Check if a row is completely empty
   308→     */
   309→    private fun isRowEmpty(row: Row): Boolean {
   310→        for (cellIndex in 0 until row.lastCellNum) {
   311→            val cell = row.getCell(cellIndex)
   312→            if (cell != null && cell.cellType != CellType.BLANK) {
   313→                val value = getCellValueAsString(cell).trim()
   314→                if (value.isNotEmpty()) {
   315→                    return false
   316→                }
   317→            }
   318→        }
   319→        return true
   320→    }
   321→}

1→package com.secman.cli.service

     5→import com.fasterxml.jackson.module.kotlin.readValue
     6→import com.secman.domain.MappingStatus
     7→import com.secman.domain.User

     9→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
    10→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
    11→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    12→import org.apache.commons.csv.CSVFormat
    13→import org.apache.commons.csv.CSVParser
    14→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    15→import java.io.File
    16→import java.io.FileReader

    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * CLI service for user mapping operations (Feature 049)
    21→ *
    22→ * Provides business logic for creating, listing, and removing user mappings
    23→ * via CLI commands. Handles validation, duplicate detection, and audit logging.
    24→ *
    25→ * Related to: Feature 042 (Future User Mappings), Feature 049 (CLI User Mapping)
    26→ */
    27→@Singleton
    28→class UserMappingCliService(
    29→    private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository,
    30→    private val userRepository: UserRepository
    31→) {
    32→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(UserMappingCliService::class.java)
    33→
    34→    companion object {
    35→        const val MAX_IMPORT_ROWS = 100_000
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    // Validation regex patterns (from spec FR-003, FR-004, FR-006)
    39→    private val emailRegex = Regex("^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$")
    40→    private val awsAccountIdRegex = Regex("^\\d{12}$")
    41→    private val domainRegex = Regex("^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+$")
    42→
    43→    /**
    44→     * Add domain-to-user mappings
    45→     *
    46→     * Creates n×m mappings (cross product of emails and domains)
    47→     * Handles duplicate detection, user existence check, and pending mappings
    48→     *
    49→     * @param emails List of user email addresses
    50→     * @param domains List of AD domain names
    51→     * @param adminEmail Admin user executing the command (for audit logging)
    52→     * @return Result summary (created, skipped, errors)
    53→     */
    54→    fun addDomainMappings(
    55→        emails: List<String>,
    56→        domains: List<String>,
    57→        adminEmail: String
    58→    ): MappingResult {
    59→        val results = mutableListOf<MappingOperationResult>()
    60→
    61→        // Validate inputs
    62→        val invalidEmails = emails.filter { !emailRegex.matches(it) }
    63→        if (invalidEmails.isNotEmpty()) {
    64→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid email format: ${invalidEmails.joinToString()}")
    65→        }
    66→
    67→        val invalidDomains = domains.filter { !domainRegex.matches(it) }
    68→        if (invalidDomains.isNotEmpty()) {
    69→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid domain format: ${invalidDomains.joinToString()}")
    70→        }
    71→
    72→        // Create cross-product mappings
    73→        for (email in emails) {
    74→            for (domain in domains) {
    75→                val result = createMapping(
    76→                    email = email.lowercase().trim(),
    77→                    domain = domain.lowercase().trim(),
    78→                    awsAccountId = null,
    79→                    adminEmail = adminEmail,
    80→                    operationType = "CREATE_DOMAIN_MAPPING"
    81→                )
    82→                results.add(result)
    83→            }
    84→        }
    85→
    86→        return summarizeResults(results)
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    /**
    90→     * Add AWS-account-to-user mappings
    91→     *
    92→     * Creates n×m mappings (cross product of emails and AWS accounts)
    93→     * Validates 12-digit AWS account ID format
    94→     *
    95→     * @param emails List of user email addresses
    96→     * @param awsAccountIds List of AWS account IDs (12 digits)
    97→     * @param adminEmail Admin user executing the command
    98→     * @return Result summary
    99→     */
   100→    fun addAwsAccountMappings(
   101→        emails: List<String>,
   102→        awsAccountIds: List<String>,
   103→        adminEmail: String
   104→    ): MappingResult {
   105→        val results = mutableListOf<MappingOperationResult>()
   106→
   107→        // Validate inputs
   108→        val invalidEmails = emails.filter { !emailRegex.matches(it) }
   109→        if (invalidEmails.isNotEmpty()) {
   110→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid email format: ${invalidEmails.joinToString()}")
   111→        }
   112→
   113→        val invalidAccounts = awsAccountIds.filter { !awsAccountIdRegex.matches(it) }
   114→        if (invalidAccounts.isNotEmpty()) {
   115→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid AWS account ID (must be 12 digits): ${invalidAccounts.joinToString()}")
   116→        }
   117→
   118→        // Create cross-product mappings
   119→        for (email in emails) {
   120→            for (accountId in awsAccountIds) {
   121→                val result = createMapping(
   122→                    email = email.lowercase().trim(),
   123→                    domain = null,
   124→                    awsAccountId = accountId.trim(),
   125→                    adminEmail = adminEmail,
   126→                    operationType = "CREATE_AWS_MAPPING"
   127→                )
   128→                results.add(result)
   129→            }
   130→        }
   131→
   132→        return summarizeResults(results)
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    /**
   136→     * Create a single user mapping with duplicate detection and status determination
   137→     *
   138→     * @return Result indicating CREATED, SKIPPED_DUPLICATE, or error
   139→     */
   140→    private fun createMapping(
   141→        email: String,
   142→        domain: String?,
   143→        awsAccountId: String?,
   144→        adminEmail: String,
   145→        operationType: String
   146→    ): MappingOperationResult {
   147→        try {
   148→            // Check for duplicate
   149→            val exists = userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   150→                email, awsAccountId, domain
   151→            )
   152→
   153→            if (exists) {
   154→                log.debug("Skipping duplicate mapping: email=$email, domain=$domain, awsAccountId=$awsAccountId")
   155→                return MappingOperationResult(
   156→                    success = true,
   157→                    operation = "SKIPPED_DUPLICATE",
   158→                    message = "Mapping already exists",
   159→                    email = email,
   160→                    domain = domain,
   161→                    awsAccountId = awsAccountId
   162→                )
   163→            }
   164→
   165→            // Check if user exists
   166→            val user = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(email).orElse(null)
   167→            val status = if (user != null) MappingStatus.ACTIVE else MappingStatus.PENDING
   168→
   169→            // Create mapping
   170→            val mapping = UserMapping(
   171→                email = email,
   172→                user = user,
   173→                domain = domain,
   174→                awsAccountId = awsAccountId,
   175→                ipAddress = null,
   176→                status = status
   177→            )
   178→
   179→            if (user != null) {
   180→                mapping.appliedAt = Instant.now()
   181→            }
   182→
   183→            userMappingRepository.save(mapping)
   184→
   185→            // Audit log
   186→            log.info(
   187→                "AUDIT: operation=$operationType, actor=$adminEmail, " +
   188→                "email=$email, domain=$domain, awsAccountId=$awsAccountId, status=$status"
   189→            )
   190→
   191→            val messageStatus = if (status == MappingStatus.PENDING) " (pending - user not found)" else ""
   192→            return MappingOperationResult(
   193→                success = true,
   194→                operation = "CREATED",
   195→                message = "Created$messageStatus",
   196→                email = email,
   197→                domain = domain,
   198→                awsAccountId = awsAccountId,
   199→                isPending = status == MappingStatus.PENDING
   200→            )
   201→
   202→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   203→            log.error("Failed to create mapping: email=$email, domain=$domain, awsAccountId=$awsAccountId", e)
   204→            return MappingOperationResult(
   205→                success = false,
   206→                operation = "ERROR",
   207→                message = e.message ?: "Unknown error",
   208→                email = email,
   209→                domain = domain,
   210→                awsAccountId = awsAccountId
   211→            )
   212→        }
   213→    }
   214→
   215→    /**
   216→     * List user mappings with optional filtering
   217→     *
   218→     * @param email Filter by specific email (optional, null = all users)
   219→     * @param status Filter by status (ACTIVE, PENDING, or null = all statuses)
   220→     * @return List of user mappings matching criteria
   221→     */
   222→    fun listMappings(
   223→        email: String? = null,
   224→        status: MappingStatus? = null
   225→    ): List<UserMapping> {
   226→        return when {
   227→            email != null && status != null -> {
   228→                // Filter by both email and status
   229→                userMappingRepository.findByEmailAndStatus(email.lowercase().trim(), status)
   230→            }
   231→            email != null -> {
   232→                // Filter by email only
   233→                userMappingRepository.findByEmail(email.lowercase().trim())
   234→            }
   235→            status != null -> {
   236→                // Filter by status only - need to get all and filter
   237→                userMappingRepository.findAll().filter { it.status == status }
   238→            }
   239→            else -> {
   240→                // No filters - return all
   241→                userMappingRepository.findAll().toList()
   242→            }
   243→        }
   244→    }
   245→
   246→    /**
   247→     * Remove user mappings based on criteria
   248→     *
   249→     * @param email User email (required)
   250→     * @param domain Specific domain to remove (optional)
   251→     * @param awsAccountId Specific AWS account to remove (optional)
   252→     * @param removeAll Remove all mappings for user (default: false)
   253→     * @param adminEmail Admin user executing the command
   254→     * @return Number of mappings removed
   255→     */
   256→    fun removeMappings(
   257→        email: String,
   258→        domain: String? = null,
   259→        awsAccountId: String? = null,
   260→        removeAll: Boolean = false,
   261→        adminEmail: String
   262→    ): Int {
   263→        val normalizedEmail = email.lowercase().trim()
   264→
   265→        // Find mappings to delete
   266→        val mappingsToDelete = when {
   267→            removeAll -> {
   268→                // Remove all mappings for this email
   269→                userMappingRepository.findByEmail(normalizedEmail)
   270→            }
   271→            domain != null -> {
   272→                // Remove specific domain mapping
   273→                val mapping = userMappingRepository.findByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   274→                    normalizedEmail, null, domain.lowercase().trim()
   275→                )
   276→                if (mapping.isPresent) listOf(mapping.get()) else emptyList()
   277→            }
   278→            awsAccountId != null -> {
   279→                // Remove specific AWS account mapping
   280→                val mapping = userMappingRepository.findByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   281→                    normalizedEmail, awsAccountId.trim(), null
   282→                )
   283→                if (mapping.isPresent) listOf(mapping.get()) else emptyList()
   284→            }
   285→            else -> {
   286→                throw IllegalArgumentException(
   287→                    "Must specify either --domain, --account, or --all to indicate what to remove"
   288→                )
   289→            }
   290→        }
   291→
   292→        if (mappingsToDelete.isEmpty()) {
   293→            throw IllegalArgumentException("No mappings found matching the specified criteria")
   294→        }
   295→
   296→        // Delete mappings
   297→        userMappingRepository.deleteAll(mappingsToDelete)
   298→
   299→        // Audit log
   300→        mappingsToDelete.forEach { mapping ->
   301→            log.info(
   302→                "AUDIT: operation=DELETE_MAPPING, actor=$adminEmail, " +
   303→                "email=${mapping.email}, domain=${mapping.domain}, awsAccountId=${mapping.awsAccountId}"
   304→            )
   305→        }
   306→
   307→        return mappingsToDelete.size
   308→    }
   309→
   310→    /**
   311→     * Import user mappings from CSV or JSON file
   312→     *
   313→     * @param filePath Path to file
   314→     * @param format Format (CSV, JSON, or AUTO for auto-detection)
   315→     * @param dryRun If true, validate without saving
   316→     * @param adminEmail Admin user executing the command
   317→     * @return Result summary
   318→     */
   319→    fun importMappingsFromFile(
   320→        filePath: String,
   321→        format: String,
   322→        dryRun: Boolean,
   323→        adminEmail: String
   324→    ): MappingResult {
   325→        val file = File(filePath)
   326→        if (!file.exists()) {
   327→            throw IllegalArgumentException("File not found: $filePath")
   328→        }
   329→
   330→        // Auto-detect format if needed
   331→        val detectedFormat = when {
   332→            format.uppercase() != "AUTO" -> format.uppercase()
   333→            filePath.endsWith(".csv", ignoreCase = true) -> "CSV"
   334→            filePath.endsWith(".json", ignoreCase = true) -> "JSON"
   335→            else -> {
   336→                // Try to detect by reading first few bytes
   337→                val content = file.readText()
   338→                when {
   339→                    content.trim().startsWith("[") || content.trim().startsWith("{") -> "JSON"
   340→                    else -> "CSV"
   341→                }
   342→            }
   343→        }
   344→
   345→        log.info("Importing from $filePath (format: $detectedFormat, dryRun: $dryRun)")
   346→
   347→        val result = when (detectedFormat) {
   348→            "CSV" -> importFromCsv(file, dryRun, adminEmail)
   349→            "JSON" -> importFromJson(file, dryRun, adminEmail)
   350→            else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Unsupported format: $detectedFormat")
   351→        }
   352→
   353→        if (dryRun) {
   354→            val comparison = compareMappingsWithDb(result.operations)
   355→            return result.copy(comparison = comparison)
   356→        }
   357→
   358→        return result
   359→    }
   360→
   361→    /**
   362→     * Import mappings from CSV file
   363→     *
   364→     * Expected CSV format:
   365→     * email,type,value
   366→     * user@example.com,DOMAIN,example.com
   367→     * user@example.com,AWS_ACCOUNT,123456789012
   368→     */
   369→    private fun importFromCsv(file: File, dryRun: Boolean, adminEmail: String): MappingResult {
   370→        val results = mutableListOf<MappingOperationResult>()
   371→        var lineNumber = 1
   372→
   373→        try {
   374→            FileReader(file).use { reader ->
   375→                val csvParser = CSVParser(
   376→                    reader,
   377→                    CSVFormat.DEFAULT.builder()
   378→                        .setHeader()
   379→                        .setSkipHeaderRecord(true)
   380→                        .setIgnoreEmptyLines(true)
   381→                        .setTrim(true)
   382→                        .build()
   383→                )
   384→
   385→                csvParser.forEach { record ->
   386→                    lineNumber++
   387→                    if (lineNumber - 1 > MAX_IMPORT_ROWS) {
   388→                        throw IllegalArgumentException(
   389→                            "File exceeds maximum of $MAX_IMPORT_ROWS records. " +
   390→                            "Split the file into smaller batches."
   391→                        )
   392→                    }
   393→                    try {
   394→                        val email = record.get("email") ?: record.get("Email") ?: record.get("EMAIL")
   395→                        val type = record.get("type") ?: record.get("Type") ?: record.get("TYPE")
   396→                        val value = record.get("value") ?: record.get("Value") ?: record.get("VALUE")
   397→
   398→                        if (email.isNullOrBlank() || type.isNullOrBlank() || value.isNullOrBlank()) {
   399→                            results.add(
   400→                                MappingOperationResult(
   401→                                    success = false,
   402→                                    operation = "ERROR",
   403→                                    message = "Line $lineNumber: Missing required fields (email, type, value)",
   404→                                    email = email ?: "",
   405→                                    domain = null,
   406→                                    awsAccountId = null
   407→                                )
   408→                            )
   409→                            return@forEach
   410→                        }
   411→
   412→                        val result = when (type.uppercase()) {
   413→                            "DOMAIN" -> {
   414→                                if (!dryRun) {
   415→                                    createMapping(
   416→                                        email = email.trim(),
   417→                                        domain = value.trim(),
   418→                                        awsAccountId = null,
   419→                                        adminEmail = adminEmail,
   420→                                        operationType = "IMPORT_CSV_DOMAIN"
   421→                                    )
   422→                                } else {
   423→                                    // Dry-run validation
   424→                                    if (!emailRegex.matches(email.trim())) {
   425→                                        MappingOperationResult(
   426→                                            success = false,
   427→                                            operation = "ERROR",
   428→                                            message = "Line $lineNumber: Invalid email format",
   429→                                            email = email.trim(),
   430→                                            domain = value.trim()
   431→                                        )
   432→                                    } else if (!domainRegex.matches(value.trim())) {
   433→                                        MappingOperationResult(
   434→                                            success = false,
   435→                                            operation = "ERROR",
   436→                                            message = "Line $lineNumber: Invalid domain format",
   437→                                            email = email.trim(),
   438→                                            domain = value.trim()
   439→                                        )
   440→                                    } else {
   441→                                        MappingOperationResult(
   442→                                            success = true,
   443→                                            operation = "WOULD_CREATE",
   444→                                            message = "Would create mapping (dry-run)",
   445→                                            email = email.trim(),
   446→                                            domain = value.trim()
   447→                                        )
   448→                                    }
   449→                                }
   450→                            }
   451→                            "AWS_ACCOUNT" -> {
   452→                                if (!dryRun) {
   453→                                    createMapping(
   454→                                        email = email.trim(),
   455→                                        domain = null,
   456→                                        awsAccountId = value.trim(),
   457→                                        adminEmail = adminEmail,
   458→                                        operationType = "IMPORT_CSV_AWS"
   459→                                    )
   460→                                } else {
   461→                                    // Dry-run validation
   462→                                    if (!emailRegex.matches(email.trim())) {
   463→                                        MappingOperationResult(
   464→                                            success = false,
   465→                                            operation = "ERROR",
   466→                                            message = "Line $lineNumber: Invalid email format",
   467→                                            email = email.trim(),
   468→                                            awsAccountId = value.trim()
   469→                                        )
   470→                                    } else if (!awsAccountIdRegex.matches(value.trim())) {
   471→                                        MappingOperationResult(
   472→                                            success = false,
   473→                                            operation = "ERROR",
   474→                                            message = "Line $lineNumber: Invalid AWS account ID (must be 12 digits)",
   475→                                            email = email.trim(),
   476→                                            awsAccountId = value.trim()
   477→                                        )
   478→                                    } else {
   479→                                        MappingOperationResult(
   480→                                            success = true,
   481→                                            operation = "WOULD_CREATE",
   482→                                            message = "Would create mapping (dry-run)",
   483→                                            email = email.trim(),
   484→                                            awsAccountId = value.trim()
   485→                                        )
   486→                                    }
   487→                                }
   488→                            }
   489→                            else -> {
   490→                                MappingOperationResult(
   491→                                    success = false,
   492→                                    operation = "ERROR",
   493→                                    message = "Line $lineNumber: Invalid type '$type' (must be DOMAIN or AWS_ACCOUNT)",
   494→                                    email = email.trim(),
   495→                                    domain = null,
   496→                                    awsAccountId = null
   497→                                )
   498→                            }
   499→                        }
   500→                        results.add(result)
   501→
   502→                    } catch (e: Exception) {
   503→                        results.add(
   504→                            MappingOperationResult(
   505→                                success = false,
   506→                                operation = "ERROR",
   507→                                message = "Line $lineNumber: ${e.message}",
   508→                                email = "",
   509→                                domain = null,
   510→                                awsAccountId = null
   511→                            )
   512→                        )
   513→                    }
   514→                }
   515→            }
   516→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   517→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Failed to parse CSV file: ${e.message}", e)
   518→        }
   519→
   520→        return summarizeResults(results)
   521→    }
   522→
   523→    /**
   524→     * Import mappings from JSON file
   525→     *
   526→     * Expected JSON format:
   527→     * [
   528→     *   {
   529→     *     "email": "user@example.com",
   530→     *     "domains": ["example.com", "corp.local"],
   531→     *     "awsAccounts": ["123456789012"]
   532→     *   }
   533→     * ]
   534→     */
   535→    private fun importFromJson(file: File, dryRun: Boolean, adminEmail: String): MappingResult {
   536→        val results = mutableListOf<MappingOperationResult>()
   537→        val objectMapper = jacksonObjectMapper()
   538→
   539→        try {
   540→            // Handle both JSON array root and object root (e.g. {"accounts": [...]})
   541→            val rootNode = objectMapper.readTree(file)
   542→            val typeRef = object : TypeReference<List<Map<String, Any>>>() {}
   543→            val mappingsData: List<Map<String, Any>> = when {
   544→                rootNode.isArray -> objectMapper.convertValue(rootNode, typeRef)
   545→                rootNode.isObject -> {
   546→                    // Look for first array-valued field as the data source
   547→                    val arrayField = rootNode.fields().asSequence()
   548→                        .firstOrNull { it.value.isArray }
   549→                    if (arrayField != null) {
   550→                        log.info("JSON root is an object; using '${arrayField.key}' array (${arrayField.value.size()} entries)")
   551→                        objectMapper.convertValue(arrayField.value, typeRef)
   552→                    } else {
   553→                        // Single object — wrap in a list
   554→                        listOf(objectMapper.convertValue(rootNode, object : TypeReference<Map<String, Any>>() {}))
   555→                    }
   556→                }
   557→                else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("JSON root must be an array or object")
   558→            }
   559→
   560→            if (mappingsData.size > MAX_IMPORT_ROWS) {
   561→                throw IllegalArgumentException(
   562→                    "File contains ${mappingsData.size} records, exceeding maximum of $MAX_IMPORT_ROWS. " +
   563→                    "Split the file into smaller batches."
   564→                )
   565→            }
   566→
   567→            mappingsData.forEach { mapping ->
   568→                val email = (mapping["email"] ?: mapping["Email"]) as? String
   569→                val domains = (mapping["domains"] ?: mapping["Domains"]) as? List<*>
   570→                // Support both standard format ("awsAccounts": ["123..."]) and
   571→                // Cloud Custodian format ("account_id": "123...")
   572→                val awsAccounts = (mapping["awsAccounts"] ?: mapping["AwsAccounts"]) as? List<*>
   573→                    ?: (mapping["account_id"] as? String)?.let { listOf(it) }
   574→
   575→                if (email.isNullOrBlank()) {
   576→                    results.add(
   577→                        MappingOperationResult(
   578→                            success = false,
   579→                            operation = "ERROR",
   580→                            message = "Missing or invalid email field",
   581→                            email = "",
   582→                            domain = null,
   583→                            awsAccountId = null
   584→                        )
   585→                    )
   586→                    return@forEach
   587→                }
   588→
   589→                // Process domains
   590→                domains?.forEach { domain ->
   591→                    val domainStr = domain.toString()
   592→                    val result = if (!dryRun) {
   593→                        createMapping(
   594→                            email = email.trim(),
   595→                            domain = domainStr.trim(),
   596→                            awsAccountId = null,
   597→                            adminEmail = adminEmail,
   598→                            operationType = "IMPORT_JSON_DOMAIN"
   599→                        )
   600→                    } else {
   601→                        if (!emailRegex.matches(email.trim())) {
   602→                            MappingOperationResult(
   603→                                success = false,
   604→                                operation = "ERROR",
   605→                                message = "Invalid email format",
   606→                                email = email.trim(),
   607→                                domain = domainStr.trim()
   608→                            )
   609→                        } else if (!domainRegex.matches(domainStr.trim())) {
   610→                            MappingOperationResult(
   611→                                success = false,
   612→                                operation = "ERROR",
   613→                                message = "Invalid domain format",
   614→                                email = email.trim(),
   615→                                domain = domainStr.trim()
   616→                            )
   617→                        } else {
   618→                            MappingOperationResult(
   619→                                success = true,
   620→                                operation = "WOULD_CREATE",
   621→                                message = "Would create mapping (dry-run)",
   622→                                email = email.trim(),
   623→                                domain = domainStr.trim()
   624→                            )
   625→                        }
   626→                    }
   627→                    results.add(result)
   628→                }
   629→
   630→                // Process AWS accounts
   631→                awsAccounts?.forEach { account ->
   632→                    val accountStr = account.toString()
   633→                    val result = if (!dryRun) {
   634→                        createMapping(
   635→                            email = email.trim(),
   636→                            domain = null,
   637→                            awsAccountId = accountStr.trim(),
   638→                            adminEmail = adminEmail,
   639→                            operationType = "IMPORT_JSON_AWS"
   640→                        )
   641→                    } else {
   642→                        if (!emailRegex.matches(email.trim())) {
   643→                            MappingOperationResult(
   644→                                success = false,
   645→                                operation = "ERROR",
   646→                                message = "Invalid email format",
   647→                                email = email.trim(),
   648→                                awsAccountId = accountStr.trim()
   649→                            )
   650→                        } else if (!awsAccountIdRegex.matches(accountStr.trim())) {
   651→                            MappingOperationResult(
   652→                                success = false,
   653→                                operation = "ERROR",
   654→                                message = "Invalid AWS account ID (must be 12 digits)",
   655→                                email = email.trim(),
   656→                                awsAccountId = accountStr.trim()
   657→                            )
   658→                        } else {
   659→                            MappingOperationResult(
   660→                                success = true,
   661→                                operation = "WOULD_CREATE",
   662→                                message = "Would create mapping (dry-run)",
   663→                                email = email.trim(),
   664→                                awsAccountId = accountStr.trim()
   665→                            )
   666→                        }
   667→                    }
   668→                    results.add(result)
   669→                }
   670→            }
   671→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   672→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Failed to parse JSON file: ${e.message}", e)
   673→        }
   674→
   675→        return summarizeResults(results)
   676→    }
   677→
   678→    /**
   679→     * Compare parsed file mappings against existing DB mappings (dry-run enhancement)
   680→     *
   681→     * Uses normalized key tuple (email, awsAccountId, domain) matching the entity's
   682→     * @PrePersist normalization and unique constraint. Falls back gracefully if DB
   683→     * is unreachable (initialization-fail-timeout: -1 allows startup without DB).
   684→     */
   685→    private fun compareMappingsWithDb(operations: List<MappingOperationResult>): MappingComparisonResult {
   686→        try {
   687→            // Build file keys from successfully parsed operations only
   688→            val fileKeys = operations
   689→                .filter { it.success }
   690→                .map { Triple(it.email.lowercase().trim(), it.awsAccountId?.trim(), it.domain?.lowercase()?.trim()) }
   691→                .toSet()
   692→
   693→            // Load all DB mappings and filter to AWS/domain only (exclude IP-only mappings)
   694→            val dbMappings = userMappingRepository.findAll()
   695→                .filter { it.awsAccountId != null || it.domain != null }
   696→
   697→            val dbKeys = dbMappings
   698→                .map { Triple(it.email.lowercase().trim(), it.awsAccountId?.trim(), it.domain?.lowercase()?.trim()) }
   699→                .toSet()
   700→
   701→            val newKeys = fileKeys - dbKeys
   702→            val unchangedKeys = fileKeys.intersect(dbKeys)
   703→            val removedKeys = dbKeys - fileKeys
   704→
   705→            return MappingComparisonResult(
   706→                dbMappingCount = dbKeys.size,
   707→                fileMappingCount = fileKeys.size,
   708→                newCount = newKeys.size,
   709→                unchangedCount = unchangedKeys.size,
   710→                removedCount = removedKeys.size,
   711→                dbAvailable = true
   712→            )
   713→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   714→            log.warn("Could not query database for mapping comparison: ${e.message}")
   715→            return MappingComparisonResult(
   716→                dbMappingCount = 0,
   717→                fileMappingCount = operations.count { it.success },
   718→                newCount = 0,
   719→                unchangedCount = 0,
   720→                removedCount = 0,
   721→                dbAvailable = false
   722→            )
   723→        }
   724→    }
   725→
   726→    /**
   727→     * Summarize operation results into counts
   728→     */
   729→    private fun summarizeResults(results: List<MappingOperationResult>): MappingResult {
   730→        val created = results.count { it.operation == "CREATED" && !it.isPending }
   731→        val createdPending = results.count { it.operation == "CREATED" && it.isPending }
   732→        val skipped = results.count { it.operation == "SKIPPED_DUPLICATE" }
   733→        val errors = results.filter { it.operation == "ERROR" }
   734→
   735→        return MappingResult(
   736→            totalProcessed = results.size,
   737→            created = created,
   738→            createdPending = createdPending,
   739→            skipped = skipped,
   740→            errors = errors.map { "${it.email} → ${it.domain ?: it.awsAccountId}: ${it.message}" },
   741→            operations = results
   742→        )
   743→    }
   744→}
   745→
   746→/**
   747→ * Result of a single mapping operation
   748→ */
   749→data class MappingOperationResult(
   750→    val success: Boolean,
   751→    val operation: String, // CREATED, SKIPPED_DUPLICATE, ERROR
   752→    val message: String,
   753→    val email: String,
   754→    val domain: String? = null,
   755→    val awsAccountId: String? = null,
   756→    val isPending: Boolean = false
   757→)
   758→
   759→/**
   760→ * Summary of multiple mapping operations
   761→ */
   762→data class MappingResult(
   763→    val totalProcessed: Int,
   764→    val created: Int,
   765→    val createdPending: Int = 0,
   766→    val skipped: Int,
   767→    val errors: List<String>,
   768→    val operations: List<MappingOperationResult>,
   769→    val comparison: MappingComparisonResult? = null
   770→)
   771→
   772→/**
   773→ * Result of comparing file mappings against the database (dry-run only)
   774→ */
   775→data class MappingComparisonResult(
   776→    val dbMappingCount: Int,
   777→    val fileMappingCount: Int,
   778→    val newCount: Int,
   779→    val unchangedCount: Int,
   780→    val removedCount: Int,
   781→    val dbAvailable: Boolean
   782→)

    10→/**
    11→ * CLI command to import user mappings from AWS S3 bucket (Feature 065)
    12→ *
    13→ * Usage:
    14→ *   ./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 --bucket my-bucket --key mappings.csv
    15→ *   ./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 --bucket my-bucket --key data/users.json --aws-profile prod
    16→ *   ./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 --bucket my-bucket --key mappings.csv --aws-region eu-west-1
    17→ *   ./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 --bucket my-bucket --key mappings.csv --dry-run
    18→ *   ./bin/secman manage-user-mappings import-s3 --bucket my-bucket --key mappings.csv \
    19→ *       --aws-access-key-id AKIA... --aws-secret-access-key ...
    20→ *
    21→ * AWS Credential Resolution (highest priority first):
    22→ *   1. Explicit CLI flags: --aws-access-key-id + --aws-secret-access-key [+ --aws-session-token]
    23→ *   2. Environment variables: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY [+ AWS_SESSION_TOKEN]
    24→ *   3. Named profile: --aws-profile (reads ~/.aws/credentials)
    25→ *   4. Default credential chain: IAM role, SSO, etc.
    26→ *
    27→ * CSV Format:
    28→ *   email,type,value
    29→ *   user@example.com,DOMAIN,example.com
    30→ *   user@example.com,AWS_ACCOUNT,123456789012
    31→ *
    32→ * JSON Format:
    33→ *   [
    34→ *     {
    35→ *       "email": "user@example.com",
    36→ *       "domains": ["example.com", "corp.local"],
    37→ *       "awsAccounts": ["123456789012"]
    38→ *     }
    39→ *   ]
    40→ *
    41→ * Features:
    42→ * - Downloads file from S3 and imports using existing logic
    43→ * - Supports AWS credential chain (env vars, profiles, IAM roles)
    44→ * - Auto-detects format from file extension or content
    45→ * - Dry-run mode for validation without importing
    46→ * - Cron-friendly exit codes (0=success, 1=partial, 2+=fatal)
    47→ * - 10MB file size limit
    48→ * - Automatic temp file cleanup
    49→ * - Requires ADMIN role
    50→ */
    51→@Singleton
    52→@Command(
    53→    name = "import-s3",
    54→    description = ["Import user mappings from AWS S3 bucket"],
    55→    mixinStandardHelpOptions = true
    56→)
    57→class ImportS3Command(
    58→    private val s3DownloadService: S3DownloadService,
    59→    private val userMappingCliService: UserMappingCliService
    60→) : Runnable {
    61→
    62→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ImportS3Command::class.java)
    63→
    64→    @Option(
    65→        names = ["--bucket", "-b"],
    66→        description = ["S3 bucket name"],
    67→        required = true
    68→    )
    69→    lateinit var bucket: String
    70→
    71→    @Option(
    72→        names = ["--key", "-k"],
    73→        description = ["S3 object key (path to file in bucket)"],
    74→        required = true
    75→    )
    76→    lateinit var key: String
    77→
    78→    @Option(
    79→        names = ["--aws-region"],
    80→        description = ["AWS region (default: use SDK default resolution from env/config)"]
    81→    )
    82→    var awsRegion: String? = null
    83→
    84→    @Option(
    85→        names = ["--aws-profile"],
    86→        description = ["AWS credential profile name (default: use default credential chain)"]
    87→    )
    88→    var awsProfile: String? = null
    89→
    90→    @Option(
    91→        names = ["--aws-access-key-id"],
    92→        description = ["AWS access key ID (or set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID env var)"]
    93→    )
    94→    var awsAccessKeyId: String? = null
    95→
    96→    @Option(
    97→        names = ["--aws-secret-access-key"],
    98→        description = ["AWS secret access key (or set AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY env var)"]
    99→    )
   100→    var awsSecretAccessKey: String? = null
   101→
   102→    @Option(
   103→        names = ["--aws-session-token"],
   104→        description = ["AWS session token for temporary credentials (or set AWS_SESSION_TOKEN env var)"]
   105→    )
   106→    var awsSessionToken: String? = null
   107→
   108→    @Option(
   109→        names = ["--endpoint-url"],
   110→        description = ["Custom S3 endpoint URL for local testing (e.g. http://localhost:9090 for S3Mock). Also reads AWS_ENDPOINT_URL env var."]
   111→    )
   112→    var endpointUrl: String? = null
   113→
   114→    @Option(
   115→        names = ["--format"],
   116→        description = ["File format: CSV, JSON, or AUTO (default: AUTO for auto-detection)"],
   117→        defaultValue = "AUTO"
   118→    )
   119→    var format: String = "AUTO"
   120→
   121→    @Option(
   122→        names = ["--dry-run"],
   123→        description = ["Validate file without creating mappings"]
   124→    )
   125→    var dryRun: Boolean = false
   126→
   127→    @ParentCommand
   128→    lateinit var parent: ManageUserMappingsCommand
   129→
   130→    override fun run() {
   131→        var tempFilePath: java.nio.file.Path? = null
   132→
   133→        try {
   134→            println("=".repeat(60))
   135→            println("Import User Mappings from S3")
   136→            println("=".repeat(60))
   137→            println()
   138→
   139→            // Get admin user from parent command
   140→            val adminEmail = parent.getAdminUserOrThrow()
   141→            println("Admin user: $adminEmail")
   142→            println("Source: s3://$bucket/$key")
   143→            if (awsRegion != null) {
   144→                println("AWS Region: $awsRegion")
   145→            }
   146→            if (awsProfile != null) {
   147→                println("AWS Profile: $awsProfile")
   148→            }
   149→
   150→            // Resolve endpoint URL: CLI arg takes priority, then env var
   151→            val resolvedEndpointUrl = endpointUrl ?: System.getenv("AWS_ENDPOINT_URL")
   152→            if (resolvedEndpointUrl != null) {
   153→                println("S3 Endpoint: $resolvedEndpointUrl")
   154→            }
   155→
   156→            // Resolve AWS credentials: CLI args take priority, then env vars
   157→            val resolvedAccessKeyId = awsAccessKeyId ?: System.getenv("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID")
   158→            val resolvedSecretAccessKey = awsSecretAccessKey ?: System.getenv("AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY")
   159→            val resolvedSessionToken = awsSessionToken ?: System.getenv("AWS_SESSION_TOKEN")
   160→
   161→            if (resolvedAccessKeyId != null) {
   162→                println("AWS Credentials: explicit (access key ${resolvedAccessKeyId.take(4)}...)")
   163→            } else if (awsProfile != null) {
   164→                println("AWS Credentials: profile '$awsProfile'")
   165→            } else {
   166→                println("AWS Credentials: default chain (env/config/IAM)")
   167→            }
   168→
   169→            println("Format: $format")
   170→            if (dryRun) {
   171→                println("Mode: DRY-RUN (validation only, no changes will be made)")
   172→            }
   173→            println()
   174→
   175→            // Download from S3
   176→            println("Downloading from S3...")
   177→            tempFilePath = s3DownloadService.downloadToTempFile(
   178→                bucket = bucket,
   179→                key = key,
   180→                region = awsRegion,
   181→                profile = awsProfile,
   182→                accessKeyId = resolvedAccessKeyId,
   183→                secretAccessKey = resolvedSecretAccessKey,
   184→                sessionToken = resolvedSessionToken
   185→            )
   186→            println("Download complete.")
   187→            println()
   188→
   189→            // Execute import using existing logic
   190→            val result = userMappingCliService.importMappingsFromFile(
   191→                filePath = tempFilePath.toString(),
   192→                format = format,
   193→                dryRun = dryRun,
   194→                adminEmail = adminEmail
   195→            )
   196→
   197→            // Display summary (matching existing ImportCommand format)
   198→            println()
   199→            println("=".repeat(60))
   200→            println("Summary")
   201→            println("=".repeat(60))
   202→            println("Total: ${result.totalProcessed} mapping(s) processed")
   203→
   204→            if (!dryRun) {
   205→                if (result.created > 0) {
   206→                    println("Created: ${result.created} active mapping(s)")
   207→                }
   208→                if (result.createdPending > 0) {
   209→                    println("Created: ${result.createdPending} pending mapping(s)")
   210→                }
   211→                if (result.skipped > 0) {
   212→                    println("Skipped: ${result.skipped} duplicate(s)")
   213→                }
   214→            } else {
   215→                val comparison = result.comparison
   216→                if (comparison != null && comparison.dbAvailable) {
   217→                    println("Comparison:")
   218→                    println("  Backend:   ${comparison.dbMappingCount} existing mapping(s)")
   219→                    println("  File:      ${comparison.fileMappingCount} mapping(s) from S3")
   220→                    println("  New:       ${comparison.newCount} mapping(s) (in file, not in DB)")
   221→                    println("  Unchanged: ${comparison.unchangedCount} mapping(s) (in both)")
   222→                    println("  Removed:   ${comparison.removedCount} mapping(s) (in DB, not in file)")
   223→                } else {
   224→                    val wouldCreate = result.operations.count { it.operation == "WOULD_CREATE" }
   225→                    if (wouldCreate > 0) {
   226→                        println("Would create: $wouldCreate mapping(s)")
   227→                    }
   228→                    if (comparison != null && !comparison.dbAvailable) {
   229→                        println("Note: Database unavailable, comparison skipped (format validation only)")
   230→                    }
   231→                }
   232→            }
   233→
   234→            if (result.errors.isNotEmpty()) {
   235→                println("Errors: ${result.errors.size} failure(s)")
   236→                println()
   237→                println("Errors:")
   238→                result.errors.forEach { error ->
   239→                    println("  - $error")
   240→                }
   241→            }
   242→            println()
   243→
   244→            // Exit status (T019-T022: cron-friendly exit codes)
   245→            if (result.errors.isNotEmpty()) {
   246→                if (dryRun) {
   247→                    println("Validation failed (dry-run)")
   248→                } else {
   249→                    println("Import completed with errors")
   250→                }
   251→                // Exit code 1: partial success (some errors)
   252→                System.exit(1)
   253→            } else {
   254→                if (dryRun) {
   255→                    println("Validation successful (dry-run)")
   256→                } else {
   257→                    println("Import successful")
   258→                }
   259→                // Exit code 0: success
   260→            }
   261→
   262→        } catch (e: S3DownloadException) {
   263→            // Exit code 2: fatal S3 error
   264→            println()
   265→            System.err.println("ERROR: ${e.message}")
   266→            System.err.println()
   267→            System.err.println("Usage: manage-user-mappings import-s3 --bucket <bucket-name> --key <object-key>")
   268→            System.err.println("  The --bucket value must be a plain S3 bucket name (e.g. 'my-bucket'),")
   269→            System.err.println("  not a URL or ARN.")
   270→            System.exit(2)
   271→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   272→            // Exit code 2: configuration/argument error
   273→            println()
   274→            System.err.println("ERROR: ${e.message}")
   275→            System.exit(2)
   276→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   277→            // Exit code 3: unexpected error
   278→            println()
   279→            System.err.println("ERROR: Unexpected error: ${e.message}")
   280→            log.debug("Stack trace for unexpected error", e)
   281→            System.exit(3)
   282→        } finally {
   283→            // Clean up temp file
   284→            s3DownloadService.cleanupTempFile(tempFilePath)
   285→        }
   286→    }
   287→}
