   120→                    "content" to mappings,
   121→                    "totalElements" to totalElements,
   122→                    "totalPages" to totalPages,
   123→                    "page" to pageNumber,
   124→                    "size" to pageSize
   125→                )
   126→            } else {
   127→                // Get all mappings
   128→                val allMappings = userMappingRepository.findAll()
   129→                    .map { it.toResponse() }
   130→
   131→                val mappings = allMappings
   132→                    .drop(pageNumber * pageSize)
   133→                    .take(pageSize)
   134→
   135→                val totalElements = allMappings.size.toLong()
   136→                val totalPages = ((totalElements + pageSize - 1) / pageSize).toInt()
   137→
   138→                mapOf(
   139→                    "content" to mappings,
   140→                    "totalElements" to totalElements,
   141→                    "totalPages" to totalPages,
   142→                    "page" to pageNumber,
   143→                    "size" to pageSize
   144→                )
   145→            }
   146→
   147→            HttpResponse.ok(mappingsPage)
   148→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   149→            logger.error("Failed to list user mappings", e)
   150→            HttpResponse.serverError(
   151→                mapOf(
   152→                    "error" to "Internal Server Error",
   153→                    "message" to "Failed to list user mappings"
   154→                )
   155→            )
   156→        }
   157→    }
   158→
   159→    /**
   160→     * GET /api/user-mappings/{id} - Get user mapping by ID
   161→     */
   162→    @Get("/{id}")
   163→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   164→    fun getMappingById(@PathVariable id: Long): HttpResponse<*> {
   165→        logger.debug("Getting user mapping by ID: $id")
   166→
   167→        return try {
   168→            val mapping = userMappingRepository.findById(id)
   169→                .orElseThrow { NoSuchElementException("Mapping not found") }
   170→
   171→            HttpResponse.ok(mapping.toResponse())
   172→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   173→            logger.warn("User mapping not found: $id")
   174→            HttpResponse.notFound(
   175→                mapOf(
   176→                    "error" to "Not Found",
   177→                    "message" to (e.message ?: "Mapping not found")
   178→                )
   179→            )
   180→        }
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    /**
   184→     * PUT /api/user-mappings/{id} - Update user mapping
   185→     */
   186→    @Put("/{id}")
   187→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   188→    open fun updateMapping(
   189→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   190→        @Valid @Body request: UpdateUserMappingRequest,
   191→        authentication: Authentication
   192→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   193→        logger.info("Updating user mapping: id=$id, email=${request.email}, aws=${request.awsAccountId}, ip=${request.ipAddress}")
   194→
   195→        return try {
   196→            val userId = getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication)
   197→            val result = userMappingService.updateMapping(userId, id, request)
   198→
   199→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   200→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   201→            logger.warn("User mapping not found for update: $id")
   202→            HttpResponse.notFound(
   203→                mapOf(
   204→                    "error" to "Not Found",
   205→                    "message" to (e.message ?: "Mapping not found")
   206→                )
   207→            )
   208→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   209→            logger.warn("User mapping update failed: ${e.message}")
   210→            HttpResponse.badRequest(
   211→                mapOf(
   212→                    "error" to "Validation Error",
   213→                    "message" to (e.message ?: "Invalid request")
   214→                )
   215→            )
   216→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   217→            logger.warn("User mapping update failed: ${e.message}")
   218→            HttpResponse.badRequest(
   219→                mapOf(
   220→                    "error" to "Conflict",
   221→                    "message" to (e.message ?: "Mapping already exists")
   222→                )
   223→            )
   224→        }
   225→    }
   226→
   227→    /**
   228→     * DELETE /api/user-mappings/{id} - Delete user mapping
   229→     */
   230→    @Delete("/{id}")
   231→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   232→    fun deleteMapping(
   233→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   234→        authentication: Authentication
   235→    ): HttpResponse<Void> {
   236→        logger.info("Deleting user mapping: id=$id")
   237→
   238→        return try {
   239→            val userId = getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication)
   240→            userMappingService.deleteMapping(userId, id)
   241→
   242→            HttpResponse.noContent()
   243→        } catch (e: NoSuchElementException) {
   244→            logger.warn("User mapping not found for deletion: $id")
   245→            HttpResponse.notFound()
   246→        }
   247→    }
   248→
   249→    /**
   250→     * GET /api/user-mappings/domains - Get all distinct domains
   251→     */
   252→    @Get("/domains")
   253→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   254→    fun getDistinctDomains(): HttpResponse<List<String>> {
   255→        return HttpResponse.ok(userMappingService.getDistinctDomains())
   256→    }
   257→
   258→    // Feature 042: Future User Mapping Support
   259→
   260→    /**
   261→     * GET /api/user-mappings/current - List current mappings (future + active)
   262→     *
   263→     * Returns mappings that have not been applied yet (appliedAt IS NULL).
   264→     * Includes both future user mappings and active user mappings.
   265→     *
   266→     * Feature: 042-future-user-mappings
   267→     */
   268→    @Get("/current")
   269→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   270→    fun getCurrentMappings(
   271→        @QueryValue("page") page: Int?,
   272→        @QueryValue("size") size: Int?
   273→    ): HttpResponse<Map<String, Any>> {
   274→        logger.debug("Listing current mappings (future + active): page=$page, size=$size")
   275→
   276→        return try {
   277→            val pageNumber = page ?: 0
   278→            val pageSize = size ?: 20
   279→            val pageable = Pageable.from(pageNumber, pageSize)
   280→
   281→            val mappingsPage = userMappingService.getCurrentMappings(pageable)
   282→            val totalElements = userMappingService.countCurrentMappings()
   283→
   284→            val response = mapOf(
   285→                "content" to mappingsPage.content,
   286→                "totalElements" to totalElements,
   287→                "totalPages" to mappingsPage.totalPages,
   288→                "page" to pageNumber,
   289→                "size" to pageSize
   290→            )
   291→
   292→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   293→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   294→            logger.error("Failed to list current mappings", e)
   295→            HttpResponse.serverError(
   296→                mapOf(
   297→                    "error" to "Internal Server Error",
   298→                    "message" to "Failed to list current mappings"
   299→                )
   300→            )
   301→        }
   302→    }
   303→
   304→    /**
   305→     * GET /api/user-mappings/applied-history - List applied historical mappings
   306→     *
   307→     * Returns mappings that have been applied to users (appliedAt IS NOT NULL).
   308→     * These are historical records of when future user mappings were applied.
   309→     *
   310→     * Feature: 042-future-user-mappings
   311→     */
   312→    @Get("/applied-history")
   313→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   314→    fun getAppliedHistory(
   315→        @QueryValue("page") page: Int?,
   316→        @QueryValue("size") size: Int?
   317→    ): HttpResponse<Map<String, Any>> {
   318→        logger.debug("Listing applied history mappings: page=$page, size=$size")
   319→
   320→        return try {
   321→            val pageNumber = page ?: 0
   322→            val pageSize = size ?: 20
   323→            val pageable = Pageable.from(pageNumber, pageSize)
   324→
   325→            val mappingsPage = userMappingService.getAppliedHistory(pageable)
   326→            val totalElements = userMappingService.countAppliedHistory()
   327→
   328→            val response = mapOf(
   329→                "content" to mappingsPage.content,
   330→                "totalElements" to totalElements,
   331→                "totalPages" to mappingsPage.totalPages,
   332→                "page" to pageNumber,
   333→                "size" to pageSize
   334→            )
   335→
   336→            HttpResponse.ok(response)
   337→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   338→            logger.error("Failed to list applied history", e)
   339→            HttpResponse.serverError(
   340→                mapOf(
   341→                    "error" to "Internal Server Error",
   342→                    "message" to "Failed to list applied history"
   343→                )
   344→            )
   345→        }
   346→    }
   347→
   348→    /**
   349→     * Extract user ID from authentication object
   350→     */
   351→    private fun getUserIdFromAuthentication(authentication: Authentication): Long {
   352→        val userId = authentication.attributes["userId"]
   353→        return when (userId) {
   354→            is Long -> userId
   355→            is Int -> userId.toLong()
   356→            is String -> userId.toLong()
   357→            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Unable to determine user ID from authentication")
   358→        }
   359→    }
   360→}

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.IpRangeType
     4→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     5→import org.apache.commons.net.util.SubnetUtils
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Utility for parsing and validating IP addresses in various formats
     9→ *
    10→ * Supports:
    11→ * - Single IPv4 addresses (192.168.1.100)
    12→ * - CIDR notation (192.168.1.0/24)
    13→ * - Dash ranges (192.168.1.1-192.168.1.100)
    14→ */
    15→@Singleton
    16→class IpAddressParser {
    17→
    18→    /**
    19→     * Result of IP address parsing
    20→     */
    21→    data class IpParseResult(
    22→        val rangeType: IpRangeType,
    23→        val originalInput: String,
    24→        val startIpNumeric: Long,
    25→        val endIpNumeric: Long,
    26→        val ipCount: Long
    27→    )
    28→
    29→    /**
    30→     * Parse IP address string and determine type and range
    31→     *
    32→     * @param ipAddress IP address string (single, CIDR, or dash range)
    33→     * @return IpParseResult with parsed data
    34→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if format is invalid
    35→     */
    36→    fun parse(ipAddress: String): IpParseResult {
    37→        val trimmed = ipAddress.trim()
    38→
    39→        return when {
    40→            trimmed.contains('/') -> parseCidr(trimmed)
    41→            trimmed.contains('-') -> parseDashRange(trimmed)
    42→            else -> parseSingleIp(trimmed)
    43→        }
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    /**
    47→     * Validate IP address format without parsing
    48→     *
    49→     * @param ipAddress IP address string
    50→     * @return true if valid format
    51→     */
    52→    fun isValid(ipAddress: String): Boolean {
    53→        return try {
    54→            parse(ipAddress)
    55→            true
    56→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    57→            false
    58→        }
    59→    }
    60→
    61→    /**
    62→     * Convert IPv4 address string to numeric representation (Long)
    63→     *
    64→     * Example: "192.168.1.100" -> 3232235876
    65→     *
    66→     * @param ip IPv4 address string
    67→     * @return numeric representation
    68→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if invalid format
    69→     */
    70→    fun ipToNumeric(ip: String): Long {
    71→        val parts = ip.split('.')
    72→        if (parts.size != 4) {
    73→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid IPv4 format: must have 4 octets")
    74→        }
    75→
    76→        var result = 0L
    77→        for (part in parts) {
    78→            val octet = part.toIntOrNull()
    79→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid IPv4 format: non-numeric octet '$part'")
    80→
    81→            if (octet < 0 || octet > 255) {
    82→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid IPv4 format: octet $octet out of range (0-255)")
    83→            }
    84→
    85→            result = (result shl 8) or octet.toLong()
    86→        }
    87→        return result
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    /**
    91→     * Convert numeric representation back to IPv4 string
    92→     *
    93→     * Example: 3232235876 -> "192.168.1.100"
    94→     *
    95→     * @param numeric numeric representation
    96→     * @return IPv4 address string
    97→     */
    98→    fun numericToIp(numeric: Long): String {
    99→        val octet1 = (numeric shr 24) and 0xFF
   100→        val octet2 = (numeric shr 16) and 0xFF
   101→        val octet3 = (numeric shr 8) and 0xFF
   102→        val octet4 = numeric and 0xFF
   103→        return "$octet1.$octet2.$octet3.$octet4"
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    /**
   107→     * Parse single IP address
   108→     */
   109→    private fun parseSingleIp(ip: String): IpParseResult {
   110→        val numeric = ipToNumeric(ip)
   111→        return IpParseResult(
   112→            rangeType = IpRangeType.SINGLE,
   113→            originalInput = ip,
   114→            startIpNumeric = numeric,
   115→            endIpNumeric = numeric,
   116→            ipCount = 1
   117→        )
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    /**
   121→     * Parse CIDR notation (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24)
   122→     */
   123→    private fun parseCidr(cidr: String): IpParseResult {
   124→        try {
   125→            // Apache Commons Net SubnetUtils for CIDR parsing
   126→            val subnetUtils = SubnetUtils(cidr)
   127→            subnetUtils.isInclusiveHostCount = true // Include network and broadcast addresses
   128→
   129→            val info = subnetUtils.info
   130→            val startIp = info.lowAddress ?: info.networkAddress
   131→            val endIp = info.highAddress ?: info.broadcastAddress
   132→
   133→            val startNumeric = ipToNumeric(startIp)
   134→            val endNumeric = ipToNumeric(endIp)
   135→            val count = info.addressCountLong
   136→
   137→            return IpParseResult(
   138→                rangeType = IpRangeType.CIDR,
   139→                originalInput = cidr,
   140→                startIpNumeric = startNumeric,
   141→                endIpNumeric = endNumeric,
   142→                ipCount = count
   143→            )
   144→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   145→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CIDR format: ${e.message}", e)
   146→        }
   147→    }
   148→
   149→    /**
   150→     * Parse dash range (e.g., 192.168.1.1-192.168.1.100)
   151→     */
   152→    private fun parseDashRange(range: String): IpParseResult {
   153→        val parts = range.split('-', limit = 2)
   154→        if (parts.size != 2) {
   155→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid dash range format: must be 'IP1-IP2'")
   156→        }
   157→
   158→        val startIp = parts[0].trim()
   159→        val endIp = parts[1].trim()
   160→
   161→        val startNumeric = ipToNumeric(startIp)
   162→        val endNumeric = ipToNumeric(endIp)
   163→
   164→        if (startNumeric > endNumeric) {
   165→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid range: start IP ($startIp) must be <= end IP ($endIp)")
   166→        }
   167→
   168→        val count = endNumeric - startNumeric + 1
   169→
   170→        // Validate range size (max Class B: 65,536 IPs)
   171→        if (count > 65536) {
   172→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Range too large: $count IPs (max 65,536 for dash ranges)")
   173→        }
   174→
   175→        return IpParseResult(
   176→            rangeType = IpRangeType.DASH_RANGE,
   177→            originalInput = range,
   178→            startIpNumeric = startNumeric,
   179→            endIpNumeric = endNumeric,
   180→            ipCount = count
   181→        )
   182→    }
   183→}
   184→

1→package com.secman.controller
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.Norm
     4→import com.secman.domain.Requirement
     5→import com.secman.domain.UseCase
     6→import com.secman.repository.NormRepository
     7→import com.secman.repository.RequirementRepository
     8→import com.secman.repository.UseCaseRepository
     9→import com.secman.service.NormParsingService
    10→import com.secman.service.RequirementService
    11→import io.micronaut.http.HttpResponse
    12→import io.micronaut.http.HttpStatus
    13→import io.micronaut.http.MediaType
    14→import io.micronaut.http.annotation.*
    15→import io.micronaut.http.multipart.CompletedFileUpload
    16→import io.micronaut.scheduling.TaskExecutors
    17→import io.micronaut.scheduling.annotation.ExecuteOn
    18→import io.micronaut.security.annotation.Secured
    19→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
    20→import io.micronaut.security.rules.SecurityRule
    21→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
    22→import io.micronaut.transaction.annotation.Transactional
    23→import jakarta.inject.Inject
    24→import jakarta.persistence.EntityManager
    25→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    26→import org.apache.poi.ss.usermodel.*
    27→import org.apache.poi.xssf.usermodel.XSSFWorkbook
    28→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    29→import java.io.IOException
    30→import java.nio.file.Files
    31→import java.nio.file.attribute.PosixFilePermissions
    32→
    33→@Controller("/api/import")
    34→@Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
    35→@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
    36→open class ImportController(
    37→    private val requirementRepository: RequirementRepository,
    38→    private val requirementService: RequirementService,
    39→    private val normRepository: NormRepository,
    40→    private val useCaseRepository: UseCaseRepository,
    41→    private val normParsingService: NormParsingService,
    42→    private val entityManager: EntityManager,
    43→    private val vulnerabilityImportService: com.secman.service.VulnerabilityImportService,
    44→    private val masscanParserService: com.secman.service.MasscanParserService,
    45→    private val assetRepository: com.secman.repository.AssetRepository,
    46→    private val scanRepository: com.secman.repository.ScanRepository,
    47→    private val scanResultRepository: com.secman.repository.ScanResultRepository,
    48→    private val userMappingImportService: com.secman.service.UserMappingImportService,
    49→    private val csvUserMappingParser: com.secman.service.CSVUserMappingParser,
    50→    private val assetImportService: com.secman.service.AssetImportService
    51→) {
    52→    
    53→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ImportController::class.java)
    54→    
    55→    companion object {
    56→        private const val MAX_FILE_SIZE = 100 * 1024 * 1024L // 100MB - aligned with application.yml
    57→        private const val REQUIRED_SHEET_NAME = "Reqs"
    58→        private val REQUIRED_HEADERS = listOf(
    59→            "Chapter", "Norm", "Short req", "DetailsEN", "MotivationEN", "ExampleEN", "UseCase"
    60→        )
    61→    }
    62→
    63→    @Serdeable
    64→    data class ImportResponse(
    65→        val message: String,
    66→        val requirementsProcessed: Int
    67→    )
    68→
    69→    @Serdeable
    70→    data class ErrorResponse(
    71→        val error: String
    72→    )
    73→
    74→    @Post("/upload-xlsx")
    75→    @Consumes(MediaType.MULTIPART_FORM_DATA)
    76→    @Transactional
    77→    @Secured("ADMIN", "REQADMIN")
    78→    open fun uploadXlsx(@Part xlsxFile: CompletedFileUpload): HttpResponse<*> {
    79→        return try {
    80→            log.debug("Processing Excel file upload: {}", xlsxFile.filename)
    81→            
    82→            // Validate file
    83→            val validation = validateFile(xlsxFile)
    84→            if (validation != null) {
    85→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(validation))
    86→            }
    87→            
    88→            // Process file
    89→            val requirements = parseExcelFile(xlsxFile)
    90→            
    91→            if (requirements.isEmpty()) {
    92→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("No valid requirements found in file"))
    93→            }
    94→            
    95→            // Save requirements
    96→            val processedCount = saveRequirements(requirements)
    97→            
    98→            log.info("Successfully processed {} requirements from Excel file", processedCount)
    99→            HttpResponse.ok(ImportResponse(
   100→                message = "File processed successfully.",
   101→                requirementsProcessed = processedCount
   102→            ))
   103→            
   104→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   105→            log.error("Error processing Excel file", e)
   106→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   107→                .body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   108→        }
   109→    }
   110→    
   111→    private fun validateFile(file: CompletedFileUpload): String? {
   112→        // Check file size
   113→        if (file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
   114→            return "File size exceeds maximum limit of ${MAX_FILE_SIZE / 1024 / 1024}MB"
   115→        }
   116→        
   117→        // Check file extension
   118→        val filename = file.filename.orEmpty()
   119→        if (!filename.lowercase().endsWith(".xlsx")) {
   120→            return "Only .xlsx files are supported"
   121→        }
   122→        
   123→        // Check content type
   124→        val contentType = file.contentType.map { it.toString() }.orElse("")
   125→        if (!contentType.contains("spreadsheetml.sheet") && !contentType.contains("excel")) {
   126→            return "Invalid file format. Please upload a valid Excel file."
   127→        }
   128→        
   129→        // Check file is not empty
   130→        if (file.size == 0L) {
   131→            return "File is empty"
   132→        }
   133→        
   134→        return null
   135→    }
   136→    
   137→    private fun parseExcelFile(file: CompletedFileUpload): List<Requirement> {
   138→        val requirements = mutableListOf<Requirement>()
   139→        
   140→        file.inputStream.use { inputStream ->
   141→            val workbook = XSSFWorkbook(inputStream)
   142→            
   143→            // Get required sheet
   144→            val sheet = workbook.getSheet(REQUIRED_SHEET_NAME)
   145→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Required sheet '$REQUIRED_SHEET_NAME' not found")
   146→            
   147→            // Validate headers
   148→            val headerValidation = validateHeaders(sheet)
   149→            if (headerValidation != null) {
   150→                throw IllegalArgumentException(headerValidation)
   151→            }
   152→            
   153→            // Get header mapping
   154→            val headerMap = getHeaderMapping(sheet)
   155→            
   156→            // Process data rows (skip header row 0)
   157→            for (rowIndex in 1..sheet.lastRowNum) {
   158→                val row = sheet.getRow(rowIndex) ?: continue
   159→                
   160→                try {
   161→                    val requirement = parseRowToRequirement(row, headerMap)
   162→                    if (requirement != null) {
   163→                        requirements.add(requirement)
   164→                    }
   165→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   166→                    log.warn("Failed to parse row {}: {}", rowIndex + 1, e.message)
   167→                    // Continue processing other rows
   168→                }
   169→            }
   170→            
   171→            workbook.close()
   172→        }
   173→        
   174→        return requirements
   175→    }
   176→    
   177→    private fun validateHeaders(sheet: Sheet): String? {
   178→        val headerRow = sheet.getRow(0) 
   179→            ?: return "Header row not found"
   180→        
   181→        val actualHeaders = mutableListOf<String>()
   182→        for (cellIndex in 0 until headerRow.lastCellNum) {
   183→            val cell = headerRow.getCell(cellIndex)
   184→            if (cell != null) {
   185→                actualHeaders.add(getCellValueAsString(cell).trim())
   186→            }
   187→        }
   188→        
   189→        // Check if all required headers are present (case-insensitive)
   190→        val missingHeaders = REQUIRED_HEADERS.filter { required ->
   191→            actualHeaders.none { actual -> actual.equals(required, ignoreCase = true) }
   192→        }
   193→        
   194→        if (missingHeaders.isNotEmpty()) {
   195→            return "Missing required headers: ${missingHeaders.joinToString(", ")}"
   196→        }
   197→        
   198→        return null
   199→    }
   200→    
   201→    private fun getHeaderMapping(sheet: Sheet): Map<String, Int> {
   202→        val headerRow = sheet.getRow(0)
   203→        val headerMap = mutableMapOf<String, Int>()
   204→        
   205→        for (cellIndex in 0 until headerRow.lastCellNum) {
   206→            val cell = headerRow.getCell(cellIndex)
   207→            if (cell != null) {
   208→                val headerName = getCellValueAsString(cell).trim()
   209→                
   210→                // Map to standardized header names (case-insensitive)
   211→                REQUIRED_HEADERS.forEach { required ->
   212→                    if (headerName.equals(required, ignoreCase = true)) {
   213→                        headerMap[required] = cellIndex
   214→                    }
   215→                }
   216→            }
   217→        }
   218→        
   219→        return headerMap
   220→    }
   221→    
   222→    private fun parseRowToRequirement(row: Row, headerMap: Map<String, Int>): Requirement? {
   223→        // Get shortreq (mandatory field)
   224→        val shortreq = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "Short req")?.trim()
   225→        if (shortreq.isNullOrBlank()) {
   226→            log.debug("Skipping row {} - missing Short req", row.rowNum + 1)
   227→            return null
   228→        }
   229→        
   230→        // Get other fields
   231→        val chapter = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "Chapter")?.trim()
   232→        val normString = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "Norm")?.trim()
   233→        val details = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "DetailsEN")?.trim()
   234→        val motivation = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "MotivationEN")?.trim()
   235→        val example = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "ExampleEN")?.trim()
   236→        val useCaseString = getCellValue(row, headerMap, "UseCase")?.trim()
   237→        
   238→        // Create requirement
   239→        val requirement = Requirement(
   240→            shortreq = shortreq,
   241→            chapter = chapter?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() },
   242→            norm = normString?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() },
   243→            details = details?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() },
   244→            motivation = motivation?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() },
   245→            example = example?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() },
   246→            usecase = useCaseString?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
   247→        )
   248→        
   249→        // Parse and associate norms
   250→        if (!normString.isNullOrEmpty()) {
   251→            try {
   252→                val norms = normParsingService.parseNorms(normString)
   253→                requirement.norms = norms.toMutableSet()
   254→                
   255→                // Derive chapter from norm if chapter is empty
   256→                if (chapter.isNullOrEmpty() && norms.isNotEmpty()) {
   257→                    val firstNorm = norms.first().name
   258→                    requirement.chapter = extractChapterFromNorm(firstNorm)
   259→                }
   260→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   261→                log.warn("Failed to parse norms for requirement '{}': {}", shortreq, e.message)
   262→            }
   263→        }
   264→        
   265→        // Parse and create use cases
   266→        if (!useCaseString.isNullOrEmpty()) {
   267→            try {
   268→                val useCases = parseAndCreateUseCases(useCaseString)
   269→                requirement.usecases = useCases.toMutableSet()
   270→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   271→                log.warn("Failed to parse use cases for requirement '{}': {}", shortreq, e.message)
   272→            }
   273→        }
   274→        
   275→        return requirement
   276→    }
   277→    
   278→    private fun getCellValue(row: Row, headerMap: Map<String, Int>, headerName: String): String? {
   279→        val cellIndex = headerMap[headerName] ?: return null
   280→        val cell = row.getCell(cellIndex) ?: return null
   281→        return getCellValueAsString(cell)
   282→    }
   283→    
   284→    private fun getCellValueAsString(cell: Cell): String {
   285→        return when (cell.cellType) {
   286→            CellType.STRING -> cell.stringCellValue
   287→            CellType.NUMERIC -> {
   288→                if (DateUtil.isCellDateFormatted(cell)) {
   289→                    cell.localDateTimeCellValue.toString()
   290→                } else {
   291→                    // Use DataFormatter for consistent number formatting
   292→                    val formatter = DataFormatter()
   293→                    formatter.formatCellValue(cell)
   294→                }
   295→            }
   296→            CellType.BOOLEAN -> cell.booleanCellValue.toString()
   297→            CellType.FORMULA -> {
   298→                try {
   299→                    when (cell.cachedFormulaResultType) {
   300→                        CellType.STRING -> cell.richStringCellValue.string
   301→                        CellType.NUMERIC -> {
   302→                            val formatter = DataFormatter()
   303→                            formatter.formatCellValue(cell)
   304→                        }
   305→                        CellType.BOOLEAN -> cell.booleanCellValue.toString()
   306→                        else -> ""
   307→                    }
   308→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   309→                    log.warn("Failed to read cached formula result: {}", e.message)
   310→                    ""
   311→                }
   312→            }
   313→            else -> ""
   314→        }
   315→    }
   316→    
   317→    private fun extractChapterFromNorm(normName: String): String? {
   318→        // Extract chapter from norm name (e.g., "ISO 27001: A.8.1.1" -> "A.8.1.1")
   319→        val colonIndex = normName.indexOf(':')
   320→        return if (colonIndex > 0 && colonIndex < normName.length - 1) {
   321→            normName.substring(colonIndex + 1).trim()
   322→        } else {
   323→            null
   324→        }
   325→    }
   326→    
   327→    @Transactional
   328→    open fun parseAndCreateUseCases(useCaseString: String): List<UseCase> {
   329→        val useCases = mutableListOf<UseCase>()
   330→        
   331→        // Split by comma and create individual use cases
   332→        val useCaseNames = useCaseString.split(",")
   333→            .map { it.trim() }
   334→            .filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   335→        
   336→        for (name in useCaseNames) {
   337→            try {
   338→                val useCase = findOrCreateUseCase(name)
   339→                useCases.add(useCase)
   340→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   341→                log.warn("Failed to create use case '{}': {}", name, e.message)
   342→            }
   343→        }
   344→        
   345→        return useCases
   346→    }
   347→    
   348→    @Transactional
   349→    open fun findOrCreateUseCase(name: String): UseCase {
   350→        // Try to find existing use case (case-insensitive)
   351→        val existing = useCaseRepository.findByNameIgnoreCase(name).orElse(null)
   352→        if (existing != null) {
   353→            log.debug("Found existing use case: {}", name)
   354→            return existing
   355→        }
   356→        
   357→        // Create new use case
   358→        val newUseCase = UseCase(name = name)
   359→        val saved = useCaseRepository.save(newUseCase)
   360→        log.debug("Created new use case: {}", name)
   361→        
   362→        return saved
   363→    }
   364→    
   365→    @Transactional
   366→    open fun saveRequirements(requirements: List<Requirement>): Int {
   367→        var processedCount = 0
   368→        
   369→        for (requirement in requirements) {
   370→            try {
   371→                // Use service to properly assign internalId
   372→                val savedRequirement = requirementService.createRequirement(requirement)
   373→
   374→                // Force flush to ensure it's persisted
   375→                entityManager.flush()
   376→
   377→                log.debug("Saved requirement: {} with ID {}", savedRequirement.shortreq, savedRequirement.internalId)
   378→                processedCount++
   379→
   380→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   381→                log.warn("Failed to save requirement '{}': {}", requirement.shortreq, e.message)
   382→                // Continue with next requirement instead of failing entire import
   383→            }
   384→        }
   385→
   386→        return processedCount
   387→    }

   441→    /**
   442→     * Validate vulnerability Excel file
   443→     *
   444→     * Checks: file size, extension, content type, not empty
   445→     *
   446→     * @param file Uploaded file
   447→     * @return Error message if invalid, null if valid
   448→     */
   449→    private fun validateVulnerabilityFile(file: CompletedFileUpload): String? {
   450→        // Check file size
   451→        if (file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
   452→            return "File size exceeds maximum limit of ${MAX_FILE_SIZE / 1024 / 1024}MB"
   453→        }
   454→
   455→        // Check file extension
   456→        val filename = file.filename.orEmpty()
   457→        if (!filename.lowercase().endsWith(".xlsx")) {
   458→            return "Only .xlsx files are supported"
   459→        }
   460→
   461→        // Check content type
   462→        val contentType = file.contentType.map { it.toString() }.orElse("")
   463→        if (!contentType.contains("spreadsheetml.sheet") && !contentType.contains("excel") && !contentType.contains("octet-stream")) {
   464→            return "Invalid file format. Please upload a valid Excel file."
   465→        }
   466→
   467→        // Check file is not empty
   468→        if (file.size == 0L) {
   469→            return "File is empty"
   470→        }
   471→
   472→        return null
   473→    }

   528→    /**
   529→     * Upload user mapping CSV file
   530→     *
   531→     * Feature: 016-i-want-to (CSV-Based User Mapping Upload)
   532→     *
   533→     * Endpoint: POST /api/import/upload-user-mappings-csv
   534→     * Request: multipart/form-data with csvFile
   535→     * Response: ImportResult with counts (imported, skipped, errors)
   536→     * Access: ADMIN only
   537→     *
   538→     * Expected CSV format:
   539→     * - Required columns: account_id, owner_email (case-insensitive, any order)
   540→     * - Optional column: domain (defaults to "-NONE-" if omitted)
   541→     * - Max file size: 10MB
   542→     * - Supported encodings: UTF-8, ISO-8859-1
   543→     * - Supported delimiters: comma, semicolon, tab (auto-detected)
   544→     * - Scientific notation: Handles AWS account IDs like 9.98987E+11
   545→     *
   546→     * @param csvFile CSV file containing user mappings
   547→     * @param authentication Authentication context (for logging)
   548→     * @return Import response with counts and any error messages
   549→     */
   550→    @Post("/upload-user-mappings-csv")
   551→    @Consumes(MediaType.MULTIPART_FORM_DATA)
   552→    @Transactional
   553→    @Secured("ADMIN")
   554→    open fun uploadUserMappingsCSV(
   555→        @Part csvFile: CompletedFileUpload,
   556→        authentication: Authentication
   557→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   558→        val startTime = System.currentTimeMillis()
   559→        val username = authentication.name
   560→
   561→        return try {
   562→            log.info("CSV upload started: user={}, filename={}, size={}",
   563→                     username, csvFile.filename, csvFile.size)
   564→
   565→            // Validate file size
   566→            if (csvFile.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
   567→                val errorMsg = "File size exceeds maximum limit of ${MAX_FILE_SIZE / 1024 / 1024}MB"
   568→                log.warn("CSV upload rejected: {}", errorMsg)
   569→                return HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.REQUEST_ENTITY_TOO_LARGE)
   570→                    .body(ErrorResponse(errorMsg))
   571→            }
   572→
   573→            // Validate file extension
   574→            val filename = csvFile.filename.orEmpty()
   575→            if (!filename.lowercase().endsWith(".csv")) {
   576→                val errorMsg = "Invalid file type: expected .csv file, received ${filename.substringAfterLast('.')}"
   577→                log.warn("CSV upload rejected: {}", errorMsg)
   578→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(errorMsg))
   579→            }
   580→
   581→            // Validate content type (allow text/csv, application/csv, or generic octet-stream)
   582→            val contentType = csvFile.contentType.map { it.toString() }.orElse("")
   583→            if (!contentType.contains("csv", ignoreCase = true) &&
   584→                !contentType.contains("text", ignoreCase = true) &&
   585→                !contentType.contains("octet-stream", ignoreCase = true)) {
   586→                log.warn("CSV upload: unexpected content-type: {}", contentType)
   587→                // Allow anyway since browsers may send different content types
   588→            }
   589→
   590→            // Check file is not empty
   591→            if (csvFile.size == 0L) {
   592→                val errorMsg = "Empty file uploaded"
   593→                log.warn("CSV upload rejected: {}", errorMsg)
   594→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(errorMsg))
   595→            }
   596→
   597→            // Save to temporary file for processing
   598→            // Security: Use Files.createTempFile with restrictive permissions to prevent TOCTOU attacks
   599→            val tempPath = try {
   600→                // Try to create with restrictive POSIX permissions (owner read/write only)
   601→                val attrs = PosixFilePermissions.asFileAttribute(PosixFilePermissions.fromString("rw-------"))
   602→                Files.createTempFile("csv_upload_", ".csv", attrs)
   603→            } catch (e: UnsupportedOperationException) {
   604→                // Fallback for non-POSIX systems (Windows)
   605→                Files.createTempFile("csv_upload_", ".csv")
   606→            }
   607→            val tempFile = tempPath.toFile()
   608→            try {
   609→                csvFile.inputStream.use { input ->
   610→                    Files.newOutputStream(tempPath).use { output ->
   611→                        input.copyTo(output)
   612→                    }
   613→                }
   614→
   615→                // Parse CSV
   616→                val result = csvUserMappingParser.parse(tempFile)
   617→
   618→                val duration = System.currentTimeMillis() - startTime
   619→                log.info("CSV upload completed: user={}, imported={}, skipped={}, duration={}ms",
   620→                         username, result.imported, result.skipped, duration)
   621→
   622→                HttpResponse.ok(result)
   623→
   624→            } finally {
   625→                // Clean up temp file
   626→                Files.deleteIfExists(tempPath)
   627→            }
   628→
   629→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   630→            // Validation errors (missing headers, empty file, etc.)
   631→            log.warn("CSV upload validation error: user={}, error={}", username, e.message)
   632→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Invalid CSV format"))
   633→
   634→        } catch (e: IOException) {
   635→            // File I/O errors
   636→            log.error("CSV upload I/O error: user={}, error={}", username, e.message, e)
   637→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   638→                .body(ErrorResponse("Error reading CSV file: ${e.message}"))
   639→
   640→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   641→            // Unexpected errors
   642→            log.error("CSV upload unexpected error: user={}, error={}", username, e.message, e)
   643→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   644→                .body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   645→        }
   646→    }

   694→    /**
   695→     * Upload Masscan XML scan file
   696→     *
   697→     * Related to: Feature 005-add-funtionality-to (Masscan XML Import)
   698→     *
   699→     * Endpoint: POST /api/import/upload-masscan-xml
   700→     * Request: multipart/form-data with xmlFile
   701→     * Response: MasscanImportResponse with counts
   702→     *
   703→     * Default values for auto-created assets:
   704→     * - owner: "Security Team"
   705→     * - type: "Scanned Host"
   706→     * - name: null (Masscan doesn't provide hostname)
   707→     * - description: ""
   708→     *
   709→     * @param xmlFile Masscan XML file to import (max 10MB)
   710→     * @return Import response with counts (assetsCreated, assetsUpdated, portsImported)
   711→     */
   712→    @Post("/upload-masscan-xml")
   713→    @Consumes(MediaType.MULTIPART_FORM_DATA)
   714→    @Transactional
   715→    @Secured("ADMIN", "VULN")
   716→    open fun uploadMasscanXml(
   717→        @Part xmlFile: CompletedFileUpload,
   718→        authentication: Authentication
   719→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   720→        return try {
   721→            log.debug("Processing Masscan XML file upload: {}", xmlFile.filename)
   722→
   723→            val username = authentication.name
   724→
   725→            // Validate file
   726→            val validation = validateMasscanFile(xmlFile)
   727→            if (validation != null) {
   728→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(validation))
   729→            }
   730→
   731→            // Parse XML
   732→            val scanData = masscanParserService.parseMasscanXml(xmlFile.bytes)
   733→
   734→            // Create Scan entity
   735→            val scan = com.secman.domain.Scan(
   736→                scanType = "masscan",
   737→                filename = xmlFile.filename,
   738→                scanDate = scanData.scanDate,
   739→                uploadedBy = username,
   740→                hostCount = scanData.hosts.size,
   741→                duration = null  // Masscan doesn't provide duration
   742→            )
   743→            val savedScan = scanRepository.save(scan)
   744→
   745→            var assetsCreated = 0
   746→            var assetsUpdated = 0
   747→            var portsImported = 0
   748→
   749→            // Import hosts and ports
   750→            for (host in scanData.hosts) {
   751→                try {
   752→                    // Find or create asset by IP
   753→                    val existingAsset = assetRepository.findByIp(host.ipAddress).firstOrNull()
   754→                    val asset = if (existingAsset == null) {
   755→                        // Create with defaults (name = IP since hostname not provided)
   756→                        val newAsset = com.secman.domain.Asset(
   757→                            name = host.ipAddress,  // Use IP as name
   758→                            ip = host.ipAddress,
   759→                            type = "Scanned Host",
   760→                            owner = "Security Team",
   761→                            description = ""
   762→                        )
   763→                        newAsset.lastSeen = host.timestamp
   764→                        val saved = assetRepository.save(newAsset)
   765→                        assetsCreated++
   766→                        saved
   767→                    } else {
   768→                        // Update lastSeen
   769→                        existingAsset.lastSeen = host.timestamp
   770→                        assetRepository.save(existingAsset)
   771→                        assetsUpdated++
   772→                        existingAsset
   773→                    }
   774→
   775→                    // Create ScanResult for this host
   776→                    val scanResult = com.secman.domain.ScanResult(
   777→                        scan = savedScan,
   778→                        asset = asset,
   779→                        ipAddress = host.ipAddress,
   780→                        hostname = null,  // Masscan doesn't provide hostname
   781→                        discoveredAt = host.timestamp
   782→                    )
   783→
   784→                    // Import ports (only "open" already filtered by parser)
   785→                    for (port in host.ports) {
   786→                        try {
   787→                            val scanPort = com.secman.domain.ScanPort(
   788→                                scanResult = scanResult,
   789→                                portNumber = port.portNumber,
   790→                                protocol = port.protocol,
   791→                                state = port.state,
   792→                                service = null,  // Masscan doesn't provide service detection
   793→                                version = null   // Masscan doesn't provide version detection
   794→                            )
   795→                            scanResult.addPort(scanPort)
   796→                            portsImported++
   797→                        } catch (e: Exception) {
   798→                            log.warn("Failed to import port {}: {}", port.portNumber, e.message)
   799→                        }
   800→                    }
   801→
   802→                    // Add result to scan and save
   803→                    savedScan.addResult(scanResult)
   804→                    asset.addScanResult(scanResult)
   805→
   806→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   807→                    log.warn("Failed to process host {}: {}", host.ipAddress, e.message)
   808→                }
   809→            }
   810→
   811→            // Save scan with all results
   812→            scanRepository.update(savedScan)
   813→
   814→            log.info("Successfully imported Masscan scan: {} assets created, {} updated, {} ports imported",
   815→                     assetsCreated, assetsUpdated, portsImported)
   816→
   817→            HttpResponse.ok(MasscanImportResponse(
   818→                message = "Imported $portsImported ports across $assetsCreated new assets" +
   819→                         (if (assetsUpdated > 0) ", updated $assetsUpdated existing asset${if (assetsUpdated > 1) "s" else ""}" else ""),
   820→                assetsCreated = assetsCreated,
   821→                assetsUpdated = assetsUpdated,
   822→                portsImported = portsImported
   823→            ))
   824→
   825→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   826→            log.error("Error processing Masscan XML file", e)
   827→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
   828→                .body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   829→        }
   830→    }
   831→
   832→    /**
   833→     * Validate Masscan XML file
   834→     *
   835→     * Checks: file size, extension, content type, not empty
   836→     *
   837→     * @param file Uploaded file
   838→     * @return Error message if invalid, null if valid
   839→     */
   840→    private fun validateMasscanFile(file: CompletedFileUpload): String? {
   841→        // Check file size
   842→        if (file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
   843→            return "File size exceeds maximum limit of ${MAX_FILE_SIZE / 1024 / 1024}MB"
   844→        }
   845→
   846→        // Check file extension
   847→        val filename = file.filename.orEmpty()
   848→        if (!filename.lowercase().endsWith(".xml")) {
   849→            return "Only .xml files are supported"
   850→        }
   851→
   852→        // Check content type
   853→        val contentType = file.contentType.map { it.toString() }.orElse("")
   854→        if (!contentType.contains("xml") && !contentType.contains("octet-stream")) {
   855→            return "Invalid file format. Please upload a valid XML file."
   856→        }
   857→
   858→        // Check file is not empty
   859→        if (file.size == 0L) {
   860→            return "File is empty"
   861→        }
   862→
   863→        return null
   864→    }
   865→
   866→    @Serdeable
   867→    data class MasscanImportResponse(
   868→        val message: String,
   869→        val assetsCreated: Int,
   870→        val assetsUpdated: Int,
   871→        val portsImported: Int
   872→    )
   873→
   874→    /**
   875→     * Import assets from Excel file
   876→     * Feature: 029-asset-bulk-operations (User Story 3 - Import Assets from File)
   877→     *
   878→     * POST /api/import/upload-assets-xlsx
   879→     * Auth: Any authenticated user
   880→     * Request: multipart/form-data with xlsxFile
   881→     * Response: ImportResult
   882→     *
   883→     * Related Requirements:
   884→     * - FR-017: Accept Excel files with validation for file size, format, required fields
   885→     * - FR-018: Validate required fields (name, type, owner)
   886→     * - FR-019: Validate data formats (IP address, type values)
   887→     * - FR-020: Handle duplicate asset names by skipping
   888→     * - FR-021: Associate imported assets with workgroups
   889→     * - FR-022: Track importing user as creator
   890→     * - FR-023: Provide import summary
   891→     *
   892→     * Error Responses:
   893→     * - 400: Invalid file format, validation errors, missing headers
   894→     * - 401: User not authenticated
   895→     * - 500: Import failed
   896→     */
   897→    @Post("/upload-assets-xlsx")
   898→    @Consumes(MediaType.MULTIPART_FORM_DATA)
   899→    @Secured("ADMIN", "VULN")
   900→    open fun uploadAssetsExcel(
   901→        @Part xlsxFile: CompletedFileUpload,
   902→        authentication: Authentication
   903→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   904→        return try {
   905→            log.info("Asset import request from user: {} with file: {}", authentication.name, xlsxFile.filename)
   906→
   907→            // Validate file size
   908→            if (xlsxFile.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
   909→                log.warn("File size exceeds maximum limit: {} bytes", xlsxFile.size)
   910→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("File size exceeds maximum limit of 10MB"))
   911→            }
   912→
   913→            // Validate file extension
   914→            if (!xlsxFile.filename.endsWith(".xlsx", ignoreCase = true)) {
   915→                log.warn("Invalid file format: {}", xlsxFile.filename)
   916→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Invalid file format. Please upload a valid Excel file (.xlsx)."))
   917→            }
   918→
   919→            // Validate file is not empty
   920→            if (xlsxFile.size == 0L) {
   921→                log.warn("Empty file uploaded: {}", xlsxFile.filename)
   922→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("File is empty"))
   923→            }
   924→
   925→            // Validate content type
   926→            val contentType = xlsxFile.contentType.orElse(null)
   927→            if (contentType != null &&
   928→                !contentType.name.contains("spreadsheet") &&
   929→                !contentType.name.contains("excel") &&
   930→                !contentType.name.contains("application/vnd.openxmlformats")) {
   931→                log.warn("Invalid content type: {}", contentType.name)
   932→                return HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse("Invalid file format. Please upload an Excel file."))
   933→            }
   934→
   935→            // Import assets
   936→            val result = xlsxFile.inputStream.use { stream ->
   937→                assetImportService.importFromExcel(stream, authentication)
   938→            }
   939→
   940→            log.info("Asset import complete for user {}: {} imported, {} skipped",
   941→                authentication.name, result.imported, result.skipped)
   942→
   943→            HttpResponse.ok(result)
   944→
   945→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   946→            log.warn("Asset import validation error: {}", e.message)
   947→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(e.message ?: "Validation error"))
   948→
   949→        } catch (e: IOException) {
   950→            log.error("Asset import IO error", e)
   951→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   952→                .body(ErrorResponse("Failed to read file: ${e.message}"))
   953→
   954→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   955→            log.error("Asset import failed for user: {}", authentication.name, e)
   956→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   957→                .body(ErrorResponse("An internal error occurred"))
   958→        }
   959→    }
   960→}

     9→/**
    10→ * Unit tests for AddVulnerabilityCommand parameter validation.
    11→ * Feature: 056-test-suite (User Story 1 - P1)
    12→ *
    13→ * Tests CLI parameter validation:
    14→ * - Required parameter enforcement
    15→ * - Criticality enum validation
    16→ * - Days-open validation

    18→@DisplayName("AddVulnerabilityCommand Parameter Validation")
    19→class AddVulnerabilityCommandTest {
    20→
    21→    @Nested
    22→    @DisplayName("Required Parameters")
    23→    inner class RequiredParameterTests {
    24→
    25→        @Test
    26→        @DisplayName("CLI-003: Requires hostname parameter")
    27→        fun `requiresHostname`() {
    28→            val cmd = CommandLine(AddVulnerabilityCommand())
    29→
    30→            // Command should define hostname as required
    31→            val optionSpec = cmd.commandSpec.findOption("--hostname")
    32→            assertThat(optionSpec).isNotNull
    33→            assertThat(optionSpec?.required()).isTrue()
    34→        }
    35→
    36→        @Test
    37→        @DisplayName("CLI-004: Requires cve parameter")
    38→        fun `requiresCve`() {
    39→            val cmd = CommandLine(AddVulnerabilityCommand())
    40→
    41→            // Command should define cve as required
    42→            val optionSpec = cmd.commandSpec.findOption("--cve")
    43→            assertThat(optionSpec).isNotNull
    44→            assertThat(optionSpec?.required()).isTrue()
    45→        }
    46→
    47→        @Test
    48→        @DisplayName("CLI-005: Requires criticality parameter")
    49→        fun `requiresCriticality`() {
    50→            val cmd = CommandLine(AddVulnerabilityCommand())
    51→
    52→            // Command should define criticality as required
    53→            val optionSpec = cmd.commandSpec.findOption("--criticality")
    54→            assertThat(optionSpec).isNotNull
    55→            assertThat(optionSpec?.required()).isTrue()
    56→        }
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    @Nested
    60→    @DisplayName("Criticality Validation")
    61→    inner class CriticalityValidationTests {
    62→
    63→        @Test
    64→        @DisplayName("CLI-001: Accepts valid criticality values")
    65→        fun `acceptsValidCriticalityValues`() {
    66→            val validValues = listOf("CRITICAL", "HIGH", "MEDIUM", "LOW")
    67→
    68→            validValues.forEach { criticality ->
    69→                val cmd = CommandLine(AddVulnerabilityCommand())
    70→                val result = cmd.parseArgs(
    71→                    "--hostname", "test-host",
    72→                    "--cve", "CVE-2024-001",
    73→                    "--criticality", criticality
    74→                )
    75→
    76→                val command = cmd.getCommand<AddVulnerabilityCommand>()
    77→                assertThat(command.criticality).isEqualTo(criticality)
    78→            }
    79→        }
    80→
    81→        @Test
    82→        @DisplayName("CLI-007: Normalizes lowercase criticality to uppercase internally")
    83→        fun `normalizeCriticalityCase`() {
    84→            // Note: The actual normalization happens in the service/DTO layer
    85→            // Here we test that the command accepts the input
    86→            val cmd = CommandLine(AddVulnerabilityCommand())
    87→
    88→            // Parse lowercase criticality
    89→            cmd.parseArgs(
    90→                "--hostname", "test-host",
    91→                "--cve", "CVE-2024-001",
    92→                "--criticality", "high"
    93→            )
    94→
    95→            val command = cmd.getCommand<AddVulnerabilityCommand>()
    96→            // Command stores as-is, normalization happens in backend DTO
    97→            assertThat(command.criticality).isEqualTo("high")
    98→        }
    99→    }

     2→
     3→import picocli.CommandLine.*
     4→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     5→
     6→/**
     7→ * Parent command for user mapping management operations (Feature 049)
     8→ *
     9→ * Provides subcommands for creating, listing, and removing user-to-domain
    10→ * and user-to-AWS-account mappings via CLI.
    11→ *
    12→ * Usage:
    13→ *   ./gradlew cli:run --args='manage-user-mappings <subcommand> [options]'
    14→ *
    15→ * Subcommands:
    16→ *   add-domain  - Add domain-to-user mappings
    17→ *   add-aws     - Add AWS-account-to-user mappings
    18→ *   list        - List existing mappings
    19→ *   remove      - Remove user mappings
    20→ *   import      - Batch import from CSV/JSON file
    21→ *   import-s3   - Batch import from AWS S3 bucket (Feature 065)
    22→ *   list-bucket - List objects in an S3 bucket (Feature 065)
    23→ *
    24→ * Authentication:
    25→ *   All operations require ADMIN role
    26→ *   Specify admin user via:
    27→ *   - --admin-user flag
    28→ *   - SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL environment variable
    29→ */
    30→@Singleton
    31→@Command(
    32→    name = "manage-user-mappings",
    33→    description = ["Manage user mappings for domains and AWS accounts"],
    34→    mixinStandardHelpOptions = true,
    35→    subcommands = [
    36→        AddDomainCommand::class,
    37→        AddAwsCommand::class,
    38→        ListCommand::class,
    39→        RemoveCommand::class,
    40→        ImportCommand::class,
    41→        ImportS3Command::class,
    42→        ListBucketCommand::class
    43→    ]
    44→)
    45→class ManageUserMappingsCommand : Runnable {
    46→
    47→    @Option(
    48→        names = ["--admin-user", "-u"],
    49→        description = ["Admin user email (defaults to SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL env var)"]
    50→    )
    51→    var adminUser: String? = null
    52→
    53→    @Spec
    54→    lateinit var spec: Model.CommandSpec
    55→
    56→    /**
    57→     * Get the admin user email from flag or environment variable
    58→     *
    59→     * @return Admin user email
    60→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if no admin user specified
    61→     */
    62→    fun getAdminUserOrThrow(): String {
    63→        return adminUser
    64→            ?: System.getenv("SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL")
    65→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException(
    66→                "Admin user required. Use --admin-user flag or set SECMAN_ADMIN_EMAIL environment variable"
    67→            )
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    override fun run() {
    71→        // Show help if no subcommand specified
    72→        spec.commandLine().usage(System.out)
    73→    }
    74→}

1→package com.secman.mcp.tools
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.McpOperation
     4→import com.secman.dto.mcp.McpExecutionContext
     5→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
     6→import io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable
     7→import io.micronaut.data.model.Sort
     8→import jakarta.inject.Inject
     9→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    10→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    11→import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * MCP tool for listing user mappings with pagination and filtering.
    15→ * Feature: 064-mcp-cli-user-mapping
    16→ *
    17→ * ADMIN role is required via User Delegation.
    18→ * Supports pagination and email filtering.
    19→ *
    20→ * Input parameters:
    21→ * - email (optional): Filter by email address (partial match, case-insensitive)
    22→ * - page (optional): Page number (0-indexed). Defaults to 0.
    23→ * - size (optional): Page size. Defaults to 20, max 100.
    24→ *
    25→ * Returns:
    26→ * - mappings: List of user mapping DTOs
    27→ * - page: Current page number
    28→ * - size: Page size
    29→ * - totalElements: Total number of mappings
    30→ * - totalPages: Total number of pages
    31→ */
    32→@Singleton
    33→class ListUserMappingsTool(
    34→    @Inject private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository
    35→) : McpTool {
    36→
    37→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ListUserMappingsTool::class.java)
    38→    private val isoFormatter = DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT
    39→
    40→    override val name = "list_user_mappings"
    41→    override val description = "List user mappings with pagination and filtering (ADMIN only, requires User Delegation)"
    42→    override val operation = McpOperation.READ
    43→
    44→    override val inputSchema = mapOf(
    45→        "type" to "object",
    46→        "properties" to mapOf(
    47→            "email" to mapOf(
    48→                "type" to "string",
    49→                "description" to "Filter by email address (partial match, case-insensitive, optional)"
    50→            ),
    51→            "page" to mapOf(
    52→                "type" to "number",
    53→                "description" to "Page number (0-indexed)",
    54→                "default" to 0,
    55→                "minimum" to 0
    56→            ),
    57→            "size" to mapOf(
    58→                "type" to "number",
    59→                "description" to "Page size",
    60→                "default" to 20,
    61→                "minimum" to 1,
    62→                "maximum" to 100
    63→            )
    64→        )
    65→    )
    66→
    67→    override suspend fun execute(arguments: Map<String, Any>, context: McpExecutionContext): McpToolResult {
    68→        // Require User Delegation
    69→        if (!context.hasDelegation()) {
    70→            return McpToolResult.error(
    71→                "DELEGATION_REQUIRED",
    72→                "User Delegation must be enabled to use this tool"
    73→            )
    74→        }
    75→
    76→        // Require ADMIN role
    77→        if (!context.isAdmin) {
    78→            return McpToolResult.error(
    79→                "ADMIN_REQUIRED",
    80→                "ADMIN role required to list user mappings"
    81→            )
    82→        }
    83→
    84→        // Extract parameters
    85→        val emailFilter = (arguments["email"] as? String)?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
    86→        val page = (arguments["page"] as? Number)?.toInt() ?: 0
    87→        val size = ((arguments["size"] as? Number)?.toInt() ?: 20).coerceIn(1, 100)
    88→
    89→        try {
    90→            // Create pageable with sorting by createdAt descending
    91→            val pageable = Pageable.from(page, size, Sort.of(Sort.Order.desc("createdAt")))
    92→
    93→            // Query based on filter
    94→            val pageResult = if (emailFilter != null) {
    95→                userMappingRepository.findByEmailContainingIgnoreCase(emailFilter, pageable)
    96→            } else {
    97→                userMappingRepository.findAll(pageable)
    98→            }
    99→
   100→            // Map to DTOs
   101→            val mappingDtos = pageResult.content.map { mapping ->
   102→                mapOf(
   103→                    "id" to mapping.id,
   104→                    "email" to mapping.email,
   105→                    "awsAccountId" to mapping.awsAccountId,
   106→                    "domain" to mapping.domain,
   107→                    "userId" to mapping.user?.id,
   108→                    "isFutureMapping" to (mapping.user == null),
   109→                    "appliedAt" to mapping.appliedAt?.let { isoFormatter.format(it) },
   110→                    "createdAt" to mapping.createdAt?.let { isoFormatter.format(it) },
   111→                    "updatedAt" to mapping.updatedAt?.let { isoFormatter.format(it) }
   112→                )
   113→            }
   114→
   115→            val result = mapOf(
   116→                "mappings" to mappingDtos,
   117→                "page" to pageResult.pageNumber,
   118→                "size" to pageResult.size,
   119→                "totalElements" to pageResult.totalSize,
   120→                "totalPages" to pageResult.totalPages
   121→            )
   122→
   123→            log.debug(
   124→                "MCP list_user_mappings: page={}, size={}, filter={}, total={}, actor={}",
   125→                page, size, emailFilter, pageResult.totalSize, context.delegatedUserEmail
   126→            )
   127→
   128→            return McpToolResult.success(result)
   129→
   130→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   131→            log.error("Failed to list user mappings", e)
   132→            return McpToolResult.error("EXECUTION_ERROR", "Failed to list user mappings: ${e.message}")
   133→        }
   134→    }
   135→}
   136→

1→package com.secman.mcp.tools
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.McpOperation
     4→import com.secman.domain.MappingStatus
     5→import com.secman.domain.UserMapping
     6→import com.secman.dto.mcp.McpExecutionContext
     7→import com.secman.repository.UserMappingRepository
     8→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
     9→import jakarta.inject.Inject
    10→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    11→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    12→import java.time.Instant
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * MCP tool for bulk importing user mappings.
    16→ * Feature: 064-mcp-cli-user-mapping
    17→ *
    18→ * ADMIN role is required via User Delegation.
    19→ * Accepts a list of mapping entries (email, awsAccountId, domain) and processes them in bulk.
    20→ * Supports dry-run mode for validation without persistence.
    21→ *
    22→ * Input parameters:
    23→ * - mappings (required): Array of mapping objects with email (required), awsAccountId (optional), domain (optional)
    24→ * - dryRun (optional): If true, validates without creating mappings. Defaults to false.
    25→ *
    26→ * Returns:
    27→ * - totalProcessed: Total number of entries processed
    28→ * - created: Number of active mappings created (user exists)
    29→ * - createdPending: Number of pending mappings created (user doesn't exist yet)
    30→ * - skipped: Number of duplicate mappings skipped
    31→ * - errors: List of validation errors with index, email, and message
    32→ * - dryRun: Whether this was a dry-run
    33→ */
    34→@Singleton
    35→class ImportUserMappingsTool(
    36→    @Inject private val userMappingRepository: UserMappingRepository,
    37→    @Inject private val userRepository: UserRepository
    38→) : McpTool {
    39→
    40→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ImportUserMappingsTool::class.java)
    41→
    42→    // Validation regex patterns (matching UserMappingCliService)
    43→    private val emailRegex = Regex("^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$")
    44→    private val awsAccountIdRegex = Regex("^\\d{12}$")
    45→    private val domainRegex = Regex("^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9.-]*[a-zA-Z0-9]$|^[a-zA-Z0-9]$")
    46→
    47→    override val name = "import_user_mappings"
    48→    override val description = "Bulk import user mappings (ADMIN only, requires User Delegation)"
    49→    override val operation = McpOperation.WRITE
    50→
    51→    override val inputSchema = mapOf(
    52→        "type" to "object",
    53→        "properties" to mapOf(
    54→            "mappings" to mapOf(
    55→                "type" to "array",
    56→                "description" to "List of user mapping entries to import",
    57→                "items" to mapOf(
    58→                    "type" to "object",
    59→                    "properties" to mapOf(
    60→                        "email" to mapOf(
    61→                            "type" to "string",
    62→                            "description" to "User email address (required)"
    63→                        ),
    64→                        "awsAccountId" to mapOf(
    65→                            "type" to "string",
    66→                            "description" to "AWS account ID (12 digits, optional)"
    67→                        ),
    68→                        "domain" to mapOf(
    69→                            "type" to "string",
    70→                            "description" to "AD domain (optional)"
    71→                        )
    72→                    ),
    73→                    "required" to listOf("email")
    74→                ),
    75→                "maxItems" to 1000
    76→            ),
    77→            "dryRun" to mapOf(
    78→                "type" to "boolean",
    79→                "description" to "If true, validate without creating mappings",
    80→                "default" to false
    81→            )
    82→        ),
    83→        "required" to listOf("mappings")
    84→    )
    85→
    86→    override suspend fun execute(arguments: Map<String, Any>, context: McpExecutionContext): McpToolResult {
    87→        // Require User Delegation
    88→        if (!context.hasDelegation()) {
    89→            return McpToolResult.error(
    90→                "DELEGATION_REQUIRED",
    91→                "User Delegation must be enabled to use this tool"
    92→            )
    93→        }
    94→
    95→        // Require ADMIN role
    96→        if (!context.isAdmin) {
    97→            return McpToolResult.error(
    98→                "ADMIN_REQUIRED",
    99→                "ADMIN role required to import user mappings"
   100→            )
   101→        }
   102→
   103→        // Extract parameters
   104→        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
   105→        val mappings = arguments["mappings"] as? List<Map<String, Any?>>
   106→        val dryRun = arguments["dryRun"] as? Boolean ?: false
   107→
   108→        if (mappings == null || mappings.isEmpty()) {
   109→            return McpToolResult.error(
   110→                "VALIDATION_ERROR",
   111→                "The 'mappings' parameter is required and must be a non-empty array"
   112→            )
   113→        }
   114→
   115→        if (mappings.size > 1000) {
   116→            return McpToolResult.error(
   117→                "VALIDATION_ERROR",
   118→                "Maximum 1000 mappings allowed per request. Received: ${mappings.size}"
   119→            )
   120→        }
   121→
   122→        // Process mappings
   123→        var created = 0
   124→        var createdPending = 0
   125→        var skipped = 0
   126→        val errors = mutableListOf<Map<String, Any>>()
   127→
   128→        mappings.forEachIndexed { index, mapping ->
   129→            val email = (mapping["email"] as? String)?.lowercase()?.trim()
   130→            val awsAccountId = (mapping["awsAccountId"] as? String)?.trim()
   131→            val domain = (mapping["domain"] as? String)?.lowercase()?.trim()
   132→
   133→            // Validate email
   134→            if (email.isNullOrBlank()) {
   135→                errors.add(mapOf(
   136→                    "index" to index,
   137→                    "email" to (email ?: ""),
   138→                    "message" to "Email is required"
   139→                ))
   140→                return@forEachIndexed
   141→            }
   142→
   143→            if (!emailRegex.matches(email)) {
   144→                errors.add(mapOf(
   145→                    "index" to index,
   146→                    "email" to email,
   147→                    "message" to "Invalid email format"
   148→                ))
   149→                return@forEachIndexed
   150→            }
   151→
   152→            if (email.length < 3 || email.length > 255) {
   153→                errors.add(mapOf(
   154→                    "index" to index,
   155→                    "email" to email,
   156→                    "message" to "Email must be between 3 and 255 characters"
   157→                ))
   158→                return@forEachIndexed
   159→            }
   160→
   161→            // Validate at least one of awsAccountId or domain is provided
   162→            if (awsAccountId.isNullOrBlank() && domain.isNullOrBlank()) {
   163→                errors.add(mapOf(
   164→                    "index" to index,
   165→                    "email" to email,
   166→                    "message" to "At least one of awsAccountId or domain must be provided"
   167→                ))
   168→                return@forEachIndexed
   169→            }
   170→
   171→            // Validate AWS account ID format if provided
   172→            if (!awsAccountId.isNullOrBlank() && !awsAccountIdRegex.matches(awsAccountId)) {
   173→                errors.add(mapOf(
   174→                    "index" to index,
   175→                    "email" to email,
   176→                    "message" to "Invalid AWS account ID format (must be exactly 12 digits)"
   177→                ))
   178→                return@forEachIndexed
   179→            }
   180→
   181→            // Validate domain format if provided
   182→            if (!domain.isNullOrBlank() && !domainRegex.matches(domain)) {
   183→                errors.add(mapOf(
   184→                    "index" to index,
   185→                    "email" to email,
   186→                    "message" to "Invalid domain format (alphanumeric with dots/hyphens, no leading/trailing special chars)"
   187→                ))
   188→                return@forEachIndexed
   189→            }
   190→
   191→            // Check for duplicate
   192→            val normalizedAwsAccountId = awsAccountId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   193→            val normalizedDomain = domain?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   194→
   195→            val exists = userMappingRepository.existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
   196→                email, normalizedAwsAccountId, normalizedDomain
   197→            )
   198→
   199→            if (exists) {
   200→                skipped++
   201→                return@forEachIndexed
   202→            }
   203→
   204→            // For dry-run, count as would-create
   205→            if (dryRun) {
   206→                val user = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(email).orElse(null)
   207→                if (user != null) {
   208→                    created++
   209→                } else {
   210→                    createdPending++
   211→                }
   212→                return@forEachIndexed
   213→            }
   214→
   215→            // Create the mapping
   216→            try {
   217→                val user = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(email).orElse(null)
   218→                val status = if (user != null) MappingStatus.ACTIVE else MappingStatus.PENDING
   219→
   220→                val userMapping = UserMapping(
   221→                    email = email,
   222→                    user = user,
   223→                    domain = normalizedDomain,
   224→                    awsAccountId = normalizedAwsAccountId,
   225→                    ipAddress = null,
   226→                    status = status
   227→                )
   228→
   229→                if (user != null) {
   230→                    userMapping.appliedAt = Instant.now()
   231→                }
   232→
   233→                userMappingRepository.save(userMapping)
   234→
   235→                if (status == MappingStatus.ACTIVE) {
   236→                    created++
   237→                } else {
   238→                    createdPending++
   239→                }
   240→
   241→                log.info(
   242→                    "AUDIT: operation=MCP_IMPORT_USER_MAPPING, actor={}, " +
   243→                    "email={}, awsAccountId={}, domain={}, status={}",
   244→                    context.delegatedUserEmail, email, normalizedAwsAccountId, normalizedDomain, status
   245→                )
   246→
   247→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   248→                log.error("Failed to create mapping for email={}", email, e)
   249→                errors.add(mapOf(
   250→                    "index" to index,
   251→                    "email" to email,
   252→                    "message" to "Failed to create mapping: ${e.message}"
   253→                ))
   254→            }
   255→        }
   256→
   257→        // Build result
   258→        val result = mapOf(
   259→            "totalProcessed" to mappings.size,
   260→            "created" to created,
   261→            "createdPending" to createdPending,
   262→            "skipped" to skipped,
   263→            "errors" to errors,
   264→            "dryRun" to dryRun
   265→        )
   266→
   267→        val modeLabel = if (dryRun) " (dry-run)" else ""
   268→        log.info(
   269→            "MCP import_user_mappings{}: total={}, created={}, pending={}, skipped={}, errors={}, actor={}",
   270→            modeLabel, mappings.size, created, createdPending, skipped, errors.size, context.delegatedUserEmail
   271→        )
   272→
   273→        return McpToolResult.success(result)
   274→    }
   275→}
   276→
